2. Grasp the handle in both hands, with your torso parallel to the band. 

3. With feet together, step outwards away from the anchor point. 

4. Bend the knee of the moving leg, reaching your hips back and descending.

name: Band Anchored Single Arm Chest Press
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at chest height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands up by your shoulders, in a "pushup" position. 

3. With one foot forward, and one back, press one of your hands outwards until your arm is fully exte

name: Band Anchored Single Arm Front Raise
description: Step on one end of the band. Lift the handle with one arm in front to shoulder height. Lower slowly.

name: Band Anchored Single Arm Incline Curl
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands down by your sides.

3. Lean your torso slightly forward, and bring one of your hands up to chest height by bending at the elbow.

4. Slowly return

name: Band Anchored Single Arm Overhead Tricep Extension
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. Grasp the band in one hand and bring your arm up overhead, so your elbow is near your ears. 

3. Bend at the elbow, bringing your hand toward your upper back.

4. Extend your hand up towards the ceiling, while keeping your elb

name: Band Anchored Single Arm Row
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at chest height, and standing facing it.

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your arms fully extended in front of you.

3. Pull one of the handles towards your armpit, while keeping an "open chest" posture. 

4. Return to the start position

name: Band Anchored Single Arm Standing Incline Press
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands up by your shoulders, in a "pushup" position.

name: Band Anchored Single Arm Tricep Kickback
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. Take the handles in both hands by your sides. 

3. Hinge at the hips, so your torso comes forward to near parallel with the floor. 

4. While keeping your elbows by your sides, drive one of your hands backwards and away

name: Band Anchored Single Arm Tricep Pushdown
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height. 

2. Grasp the handle in both hands, keeping your elbows by your sides. 

3. Extend one of your hands down towards the floor, while keeping your elbows in place. 

4. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite s

name: Band Anchored Squat to Row
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. While holding the band in both hands, do a squat by sitting back into your hips, knees, and ankles.

3. Return to the start position, then pull the handles towards your armpits, while keeping an "open chest" position.

name: Band Anchored Standing Incline Chest Press
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands up by your shoulders, in a "pushup" position. 

3. With one foot forward, and one back, press your hands outwards and upwards until your arms are full

name: Band Anchored Tricep Kickback
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. Take the handles in both hands by your sides. 

3. Hinge at the hips, so your torso comes forward to near parallel with the floor. 

4. While keeping your elbows by your sides, drive your hands backwards and away from t

name: Band Anchored Tricep Pushdown
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, keeping your elbows by your sides. 

3. Extend your hands down towards the floor, while keeping your elbows in place. 

4. Slowly return to the start position and repeat.

name: Band Assisted Chin Up
description:
1.Grab a pull up bar, palms facing you, arms and legs straightened making your body hang, foot is supported by a resistance band, the other foot wrapped around the other.

2.Pull your body upwards until your chin is just over the bar.

3.Lower your body returning to starting position.

4.Repea

name: Band Assisted Mid Grip Pull Up
description:
1.Using close grip, grab a pull up bar. Palms facing away from you, arms and legs straightened making your body hang, the foot is supported by a resistance band, the other foot wrapped around the other.

2.Pull your body upwards until your chin is just over the bar.

3.Lower your body returning

name: Band Assisted Parallel Grip Pull Up
description:
1.Grab a pull up bar in an upward fist position. Arms and legs straightened making your body hang, the foot is supported by a resistance band, the other foot wrapped around the other.

2.Pull your body upwards until your chin is just over the bar.

3.Lower your body returning to starting positio

name: Band Assisted Wide Grip Pull Up
description:
1.Using wide grip, grab a pull up bar. Palms facing away from you, arms and legs straightened making your body hang, the foot is supported by a resistance band, the other foot wrapped around the other.

2.Pull your body upwards until your chin is just over the bar.

3.Lower your body returning t

name: Band Bar Muscle Up
description:
1. Start by hanging on a bar with hands at shoulder-width, with a band secured to the bar wrapped below your foot for assistance. 

2. Slightly lean back as you pull the bar towards your torso, bringing yourself as high as possible. 

3. Once the bar contacts your body, lean forward and rotate t

name: Band Bent Over Row
description:
1. Start by standing on the band with both feet planted roughly shoulder-width apart. 

2. Reach your hips backwards, with knees slightly bent, while maintaining a long spine position. 

3. Fully extend your arms towards the floor. Pull your hands back towards your body, opening the chest and br

name: Band Bicep Curl
description:
1) Stand with both feet on resistance band holding handles long next to your sides 

2) Slowly both hands up towards your shoulders, squeezing the biceps and keeping your elbows next to your sides. 

3) Slowly lower the arms back down towards the start position, then repeat.

name: Band Calf Raise
description:
1) Grab an exercise band and stand on it with your toes making sure that the length of the band between the foot and the arms is the same for both sides. 

2) While holding the handles of the band, raise the arms to the side of your head as if you were getting ready to perform a shoulder press.

name: Band Deadlift
description:
1. Start by standing on the band with both feet set about shoulder-width apart.

2. With the ends of the band in both hands, reach your hips back with slightly bent knees and a long spine position. 

3. Drive your hips forward to return to the start position and repeat.

name: Band External Shoulder Rotation
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at hip's height. 

2. Stand with the handle in one hand, and your body parallel to the band. 

3. Bring the hand of the banded arm up so that your elbow is bent to 90 degrees. 

4. Slowly push the band outwards and away from your body, and the anc

name: Band External Shoulder Rotation (90 degrees)
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at chest height. 

2. Stand with the handle in one hand, and facing the anchor point. 

3. Bring the arm of the banded hand up so that your upper arm is parallel to the floor, and elbow bent to 90 degrees. 

4. Point your hand towards the anchor p

name: Band Face Pulls
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height. 

2. With the handles in both hands, pull the band up and towards your temples.

3. Your upper arms should be parallel to the floor, with your hands pointed towards the ceiling. 

4. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat.

name: Band Front To Lateral Raise
description:
Perform a front raise with one arm, then switch to a lateral raise. 

Alternate the movements between the front and side raises while maintaining control of the band.

name: Band Glute Kickback
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor at foot height.

2. Connect the band to your foot with an ankle strap, and use the anchor for balance. 

3. Slightly hinge forward at the hips, and drive the banded leg back and away from the anchor point.

4. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat

name: Band Internal Shoulder Rotation
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about hip height. 

2. Stand with the handle in one hand, and your body perpendicular the anchor point. Hold the band in the hand closest to the anchor point. 

3. Bring the arm of the banded hand up so that your elbow is bent to 90 degrees.

name: Band Internal Shoulder Rotation (90 degrees)
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at chest height. 

2. Stand with the handle in one hand, and facing away from the anchor point. 

3. Bring the arm of the banded hand up so that your upper arm is parallel to the floor, and elbow bent to 90 degrees. 

4. Point your hand towards th

name: Band Lat Pull Down
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor that is above head height. 

2. From a kneeling position, grasp the band with both hands. 

3. Fully extend your arms up towards the anchor point, until your elbows are straight. 

4. Pull the band all the way down until the handles are next to your sho

name: Band Lateral Raise
description: Stand on a resistance band and hold the handles with both hands. Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in the elbows, then slowly lower back down.

name: Band Lunge to Curl
description:
Stand with your forward foot on a superband, holding the band handles with both hands at your sides. 

Step back into a reverse lunge, keeping your chest upright and core engaged. While in the lunge position, perform a bicep curl by bringing the band toward your shoulders. 


Lower your hands b

name: Band Lying Leg Press
description:
1) Lay on the floor with your feet off the floor and knees bent. Place the resistance band around your feet and grab it with your hands. This will be your starting position. 

2) Execute the movement by pushing your feet out away from your body until the legs are fully extended.

3) Return to th

name: Band Overhead Reverse Lunge
description:
1. Start by standing with feet together, on the band, and the band in both hands. 

2. Press the band overhead. While maintaining this position, step one foot back into a lunge position. 

3. Push yourself up to return to the start position. Repeat on the other side.

name: Band Overhead Tricep Extension
description:
1. Start by standing on the band with feet shoulder-width apart. 

2. Take the band in both hands, press it directly overhead, then bend the elbows so that the hands come towards the upper back.

3. While keeping the elbows in this position, extend the hands upwards towards the ceiling until you

name: Band Pallof Press
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. Stand facing perpendicular to the band, holding the handles in both hands. 

3. Place your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, with knees bent. 

4. Press the handles directly outwards and away from your body witho

name: Band Pallof Press and Raise
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. Stand facing perpendicular to the band, holding the handle in both hands. 

3. Place your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, with knees bent. 

4. Press the handle directly outwards and away from your body without

name: Band Plank Row
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. Get into a plank position on your palms.

3. With the band in one hand, reach towards the anchor point until your arm is completely straight.

4. Pull the band all the way in towards your ribs. 

5. Slowly return to the star

name: Band Pull Aparts
description: Band Pull Aparts

name: Band Pull Through
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. While facing away from the anchor point, ensure that the band goes in between your legs. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.

3. Holding the handles, hinge at the hips, bringing the torso forwards and the hands back towards th

name: Band Reverse Lunge
description:
1. Start by standing with feet together, on the band, and the band in both hands at shoulder height.

2. While maintaining this position, step one foot back into a lunge position.

3. Push yourself up to return to the start position. Repeat on the other side.

name: Band Shoulder Press
description:
1) Stand with both feet on the center of the band, hip-width apart. 

2) Bring handles of the band above shoulders so that elbows are bent 90 degrees. 

3) Press arms straight up until elbows are lock. Slowly lower to the start position and repeat.

name: Band Single Arm Bicep Curl
description:
1) Stand with both feet on resistance band holding handles next to your sides 

2) Slowly curl one hand up to your shoulder, squeezing the bicep and keeping your elbow next to your side.

3) Slowly lower your arm back down to starting position. Repeat all your reps on this side before switching

name: Band Single Arm Decline Chest Press
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands up by your shoulders, in a "pushup" position. Slightly lean your torso forward.

3. With one foot forward, and one back, press one of your hands ou

name: Band Single Arm Kneeling Overhead Tricep Extension
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. Grasp the band in one hand and bring your arm up overhead, so your elbow is near your ears. 

3. Bend at the elbow, bringing your hand toward your upper back.

4. Extend your hand up towards the ceiling, while keeping your elb

name: Band Single Arm Lat Pull Down
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor that is above head height. 

2. From a kneeling position, grasp the band with one hand. 

3. Fully extend your arm up towards the anchor point, until your elbow is straight. 

4. Pull the band all the way down until the handle is next to your shoulders.

name: Band Single Arm Shoulder Adduction
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at head height or above. 

2. Stand with the band at your side, holding it with a single arm. 

3. With your arm parallel to the ground, pull the band until it is down by the side of your thigh.

4. Return to the start position, and repeat on the

name: Band Single Arm Straight Arm Pulldown
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height. 

2. With the handles in both hands, stand facing the anchor point. 

3. Holding one hand down by your side, reach the other arm straight up towards the anchor point.

4. Bring the banded arm down towards your side without bendi

name: Band Single Leg Lying Press
description:
1) Lay on the floor with your feet flat and knees bent. Raise one leg off of the ground. Place the resistance band around your foot and grab it with your hands. This will be your starting position. 

2) Execute the movement by pushing your foot out away from your body until the leg is fully extend

name: Band Split Squat
description:
1. Start by standing on the band, with feet together, while holding it in both hands at chest height. 

2. Step one foot back and plant the toes on the ground. 

3. Slowly drop the back knee towards the floor, then push back up to the starting position without stepping together. 

4. Repeat on

name: Band Standing Hip Adduction
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor at foot height.

2. Connect the band to your foot with an ankle strap.

3. Stand facing perpendicular to the anchor point, with the banded leg on the inside. Reach the banded leg towards the anchor point.

4. Return to the start position. Repeat on the

name: Band Standing Hip Flexion
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor at foot height.

2. Connect the band to your foot with an ankle strap.

3. Stand facing away from the anchor point. Drive the knee of the banded leg up towards your chest.

4. Return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

name: Band Standing Row
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at chest height, and standing facing it.

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your arms fully extended in front of you.

3. Pull the handles towards your armpits, while keeping an "open chest" posture. 

4. Return to the start position under

name: Band Standing Y's
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. Stand facing the anchor point, hold the handles in both hands, with hands by your thighs.

3. Bring your hands upwards and outwards to form a "Y" position overhead. 

4. Slowly return to the start position and repeat.

name: Band Straight Arm Pulldown
description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height. 

2. With the handles in both hands, stand facing the anchor point. 

3. Reach both arms straight up towards the anchor point, then bring them down towards your sides without bending the elbows. 

4. Reach back up, and repeat.

name: Band Tricep Kickback
description:
1. Start by standing on the band with both feet planted roughly shoulder-width apart. Hold the band in both hands next to your hips. 

2. Reach your hips backwards, with knees slightly bent, while maintaining a long spine position.

3. Bend your elbows so they come up to a position next to your

name: Band Upright Row
description:
1) Stand upright with your feet shoulder width apart and the center of an elastic band under your feet. 

2) Grasp the handles so that your palms are facing inward and touching your upper legs with your arms fully extended, elbows slightly bent. 

3) Keeping the handles close to your body, exhal

name: Band-Assisted GHD Hip Extension
description:
1. Start by securing a band to the foot pads of the GHD, and looping it around your chest, underneath your arms.

2. With your feet in the foot pads, and facing away from the feet, rest your knees on the large pad.  

3. Fully extend your legs so that your torso is parallel to the floor, with hi

name: Band-Assisted Glute-Ham Raise
description:
1. Start by wrapping a strong band around the foot pads of the GHD, and looping it around your chest.

name: Band-Assisted Hanging Leg Raise
description:
1. Start securing a band to the centre of a pull up bar, and looping it around one of your feet.

2. Grip the pull up bar at around shoulder-width. 

3. Hang with straight arms and straight legs. Lift your feet towards the bar while pressing into the bar with straight arms. 

3. Slowly return

name: Banded 2/3 SB squat followed by banded abduction
description: DO not come all the way up to standing. You want to perform about 2/3 of the lower part of a squat. then go into banded abduction until failure. This is a burn out.

name: Banded Bear Crawl
description: With a mini band around your thighs, crawl forward on hands and toes, keeping tension on the band.

name: Banded glute bridge
description: Push through heels

name: Banded Lateral Bear Crawl
description: In a bear crawl position, move laterally while maintaining tension on the band around your thighs.

name: Banded Sprinter
description: Wrap a mini band around your thighs. Assume a sprinter stance and perform fast, alternating knee drives.

name: Banded swimmer kicks with DB crunch
description: Stretch up, breathe deep and exhale to engage core. Pull up so that arms are over chest and shoulder blades are pulled down. Pull belly button into core. Begin kicks. If your core starts to flex outward. Rest for a moment and reset.

name: Banded Thruster
description: Place a mini band around your thighs. Perform a squat and press overhead, driving knees outward against the band.

name: Bar Assisted Chin Ups
description:
1. Start by securing the bar at chest height. Place your hands shoulder-width on the bar. Grip the bar with your palms facing towards you. 

2. Put your feet slightly in front of the bar, and descend down into a squat while keeping your hands on the bar. 

3. Using your arms, pull your body up t

name: Bar Assisted Pull-ups
description:
1. Start by securing the bar at chest height. Place your hands shoulder-width on the bar. 

2. Put your feet slightly in front of the bar, and descend down into a squat while keeping your hands on the bar. 

3. Using your arms, pull your body up towards the bar. You can use your legs to assist y

name: Bar Assisted Single Leg Support Pull-ups
description:
1. Start by securing the bar at chest height. Place your hands shoulder-width on the bar. 

2. Put your feet slightly in front of the bar, and descend down into a squat while keeping your hands on the bar. Reach one foot out in front, so only one foot is flat on the floor. 

3. Using your arms,

name: Bar Hang
description: 1. Grip a pull up bar at about shoulder-width, and hanging with arms and legs fully extended.

name: Barbell 21
description:
1. Stand upright and grab a barbell with an underhand grip. Place your hands shoulders-width apart and allow your arms extend toward the floor.

2. Tuck your elbows tight to the sides of your body.

3. Curl upward until you make a 90-degree angle at your elbows. Relax your arms back to full exte

name: Barbell B Stance Hip Thrust
description:
Set up as you would for a hip thrust, but stagger your feet so one foot is slightly in front of the other (B-stance). 

Drive through the back heel to lift your hips, keeping the barbell steady. Alternate stances.

name: Barbell Back Squat
description:
1) With the Barbell across your upper back, set your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Have your feet turned out slightly.

2) Keep your chest up and knees out as you sit back and down into your hips. 

3) Under control, reverse the motion and push yourself back up to a standing pos

name: Barbell Banded Hip Thrust
description:
Sit with your upper back supported on a bench, feet flat, and a barbell across your hips. 

Place a resistance band around your thighs. Drive through your heels to lift your hips, keeping your knees pushed outward against the band. 

Lower with control and repeat.

name: Barbell Behind Back Palm Up Wrist Curl
description:
1) Start by standing straight, holding a barbell behind your buttocks at arms length and shoulder width apart, with your palms facing outwards. 

2) Curl your hands up as high as possible, pause, and lower bar back to starting position. 

3) Repeat. Your arms need to be straight at all times.

name: Barbell Behind the Neck Overhead Press
description:
1.Grasp a barbell with an overhand grip and position the bar behind your neck.

2.Press the bar overhead until your arms are extended. Pause.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Barbell Bench Press
description:
1) Lie on a flat bench and start by holding a barbell above your chest with your hands slightly wider than shoulder width. 

2) Slowly lower the barbell to the middle of your chest and then press the barbell back up to the same start position until your arms are straight. 

3) Repeat.

name: Barbell Bench Press Wide Grip
description:
Lie on a flat bench and grip the barbell wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower the bar to your chest, keeping your elbows at about a 45-degree angle. 

Press the bar back up until your arms are fully extended.

name: Barbell Bent Over Row
description:
1.Stand with your feet at shoulder width and legs slightly bent. Bend over so that your back is parallel to the ground and hold the bar with palms facing up, arms straight.

2.Pull the bar up towards your lower chest. Pause.

3.Lower barbell returning to starting position. Keep your back straigh

name: Barbell Bicep Curl
description:
1) Standing straight up, grip a barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. 

2) Curl the bar up toward your chest in an arc, with your elbows locked and close to your sides. 

3) Bring the weight up as high as you can and then lower the weight slowly until your arms are straight

name: Barbell Bulgarian Split Squat
description:
1) Stand upright with your feet split front to back with the bar on the back of your shoulders.

2) Lower your body toward the floor, bending at the hips and knees and leaning your torso slightly forward with your weight even distributed between the front leg and back leg.

3) Push off the front

name: Barbell Clean and Jerk
description:
1) While performing this movement, start in a deadlift position. 

2) Ensure that your back is set, chest up, neutral or slightly arched spine. 

3) From there, you‚Äôre going to put momentum into the barbell so that you can pull it up to about chest height and scoop your hands underneath so you‚Äôr

name: Barbell Clean and Press
description:
1) Load a barbell with the appropriate weight and stand in front of the barbell with your feet shoulder width apart. 

2) To pick up the barbell, lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground and then grasp the bar with an overhand grip. 

3) Explode upwards and snap the bar to yo

name: Barbell Clean High Pull
description:
1.With the barbell on the floor, grasp the bar with an overhand closed grip with your arms straight. You will need to keep your arms straight during the whole exercise.

2.With your shoulder blades together, arch your lower back and explode off the floor upwards performing a shoulder shrug at the

name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
description:
1) Lie on a flat bench and hold a barbell with both hands in a close grip fashion. 

2) Lower the bar until it is touches your chest right below the nipples and then press the bar back up. 

3) Repeat. 

4) Keep your arms close to your sides during the whole exercise.

name: Barbell Close Grip Preacher Curl
description:
1) Kneel on the floor with your chest supported by a preacher bench, holding the barbell using a close grip and arms resting on the pad.

2) Curl the barbell up towards your shoulder. Pause.

3) Lower down the barbell returning to starting position.

4) Repeat.

name: Barbell Curtsy Lunge
description:
1. Start by standing with feet together and a barbell across your upper back.

2. Step your right foot back and across behind your left leg, planting the toe on the floor. 

3. Pushing off the left foot, return to the start position.

4. Repeat on the opposite side.

name: Barbell Deadlift
description:
1) Stand and hold a barbell with both hands in an overhand grip. 

2) With your back straight and knees locked, bend your body at your waist and lower down to the floor. 

3) Then straighten up while still holding the bar at arms length. This is a very difficult exercise and is not meant for beg

name: Barbell Decline Bench Press
description:
1) Lay back on a decline bench and hold barbell slightly wider than shoulder width. 

2) Lower the bar slightly below nipples and then press the bar back upwards. 

3) Make sure to keep your back flat on the bench at all times.

name: Barbell Decline Bench Press Close Grip
description:
Lie on a decline bench and hold the barbell with a close grip. 

Lower the bar to your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body. Push the bar back up to fully extend your arms. 

This focuses on the triceps and lower chest.

name: Barbell Decline Bench Press Wide Grip
description:
On a decline bench, grip the barbell wider than shoulder width. 

Lower the bar to your lower chest, then press it back up. 

This emphasizes the lower chest and shoulders.

name: Barbell Floor Glute Bridge
description:
1) For this exercise, maintain tight abdominals and lift by contracting your glutes. 

2) Begin seated on the ground with a loaded barbell over your legs. Using a fat bar or having a pad on the bar can greatly reduce the discomfort caused by this exercise. 

3) Roll the bar so that it is directl

name: Barbell Forward Lunge
description:
1. Stand upright with a barbell across your upper back.

2.Lunge forward with your left leg, planting the foot firmly.  Lower your body by bending at your knee and left hip,  while lowering the right knee until it almost touches the floor.

3.Return to the starting position by pushing on the lef

name: Barbell Front Rack Step Up
description:
Hold the barbell in a front rack position and step up onto a sturdy platform with one leg. 

Push through the leading leg to stand fully on the platform, then step down and alternate legs.

name: Barbell Front Racked Reverse Lunge
description:
Hold the barbell in a front rack position with elbows high. Step backward with one leg, lowering into a lunge while keeping your torso upright. 

Push through the front heel to return to the starting position and alternate legs.

name: Barbell Front Racked Split Squat
description:
Hold the barbell in a front rack position with elbows high. 

Step one foot back into a split stance and lower your back knee toward the ground. 

Push through your front heel to return to standing. Alternate legs.

name: Barbell Front Squat
description:
1) When performing this exercises, there are 2 forms of Squat. 

2) With the Barbell right above your chest, set your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Have a slight turn out of your toes. 

3) From there, you‚Äôll sit your hips back, keep your knees out, and most of your body weight

name: Barbell Full Clean
description:
1) When performing a clean, you‚Äôll start in a deadlift position. 

2) Ensure your back is set a neutral or slightly arch position. 

3) As you drive up through your legs and hips, you‚Äôll put momentum into the barbell.

name: Barbell Full Snatch
description:
1) When performing the snatch, begin by getting into a deadlift position. 

2) From there, you‚Äôll put momentum into the barbell and do a large shrug. 

3) From the shrug, propel the barbell up overhead as you get down into an overhead squat position. 

4) Ensure on the catch that you‚Äôll push y

name: Barbell Good Morning
description:
1) Stand straight with a barbell resting on the back of your shoulders with a firm grip on each side. 

2) Start by bending your body forward using your hips, until your upper body is parallel to the floor. 

3) Pause, and then raise your upper body back up. Repeat.

description:
Start with the barbell just above your knees in a hang position. 

Explosively pull the barbell up to your shoulders, catching it in a front rack position. Then, dip slightly and use your legs to jerk the bar overhead, finishing with arms locked out. 

Return the bar to the hang position and rep

name: Barbell Hang Clean Split Jerk
description:
Start in a hang position with the barbell above your knees. Perform a clean to bring the barbell to your shoulders, then dip and split your legs to jerk the bar overhead. 

Recover to a standing position and repeat.

name: Barbell Hang Snatch
description:
Begin with the barbell hanging at mid-thigh. Explosively extend your hips, knees, and ankles to pull the barbell overhead in one fluid motion. 

Catch the barbell in a squat position with arms locked out, then stand up to complete the lift.

name: Barbell High Pull
description:
1.With an overhand closed grip, lift the bar with an upright back, chest up into hanging position just below the knee.

2.With your shoulder blades together, arch your lower back and lift your legs off the floor with an explosive jump and perform a shoulder shrug at the top.

3.At the top of the

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
description:
1) For this exercise, squeeze your glutes to lift the barbell off the ground. To ensure you‚Äôre not arching your back excessively, set your abdominals tight and keep a flat back throughout the exercise. 

2) Begin seated on the ground with a loaded barbell over your legs. Using a fat bar or having

name: Barbell Incline Bench Close Grip
description:
Lie on an incline bench and hold the barbell with a close grip. 

Lower the bar to your chest while keeping your elbows close to your body, then press it back up. 

Focus on engaging your triceps and upper chest.

name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
description:
1) Sit on an incline bench set at 45 degrees. 

2) Start by holding the bar over your upper chest with your arms straight and grip slightly wider than shoulder width. 

3) Slowly lower the bar until the barbell makes contact with your upper chest. 

4) Then press the barbell straight up over y

name: Barbell Incline Bench Pull
description:
1) Lie down with your chest resting on an incline bench, holding the barbell using an overhand grip, arms straight.

2) Pull the bar upwards towards your chest. Pause.

3) Lower the barbell returning to starting position.

4) Repeat.

name: Barbell Incline Bench Wide Grip
description:
On an incline bench, grip the barbell wider than shoulder width. 

Lower the bar to your chest with control, then press it back up. This targets the upper chest and shoulders.

name: Barbell Lateral Lunge
description:
Hold the barbell on your back and step out to the side into a lunge. 

Bend the stepping leg while keeping the other leg straight. 

Push back to the starting position and alternate sides.

name: Barbell Long Stance Hip Thrust
description:
Position your feet farther from your body than in a traditional hip thrust. 

Drive through your heels to lift your hips, targeting the glutes and hamstrings. 

Lower and repeat.

name: Barbell Narrow Grip Bicep Curl
description:
1) Placing your hands closer together on the barbell, set your knees with a soft bend, hips back and chest up while you perform the bicep curl. 

2) Keep your elbows tucked in while curling.

name: Barbell Overhead Press
description:
1) Start by holding a barbell near your upper chest, in a slightly wider than shoulder width overhand grip. 

2) Press the bar overhead until your arms are extended, pause, return to start position and repeat.

name: Barbell Overhead Push Press
description:
1) Set the barbell in the front rack position, resting on the front of your shoulders with elbows high. Set your feet slightly wider than hip width. 

2) Give a slight bend in your knees and hips. Extend through your lower body, and follow through with a shoulder press overhead.

3) Your arms sh

name: Barbell Overhead Squat
description:
1) When performing the overhead squat, ensure that your hips are set roughly shoulder-width apart. 

2) Sit back and ensure that you have a safe back by keeping it neutral or slight arch to it. 

3) The barbell will be overhead, so be sure to keep a straight line lockout with your arms pointing

name: Barbell Power Snatch
description:
1) When performing the power snatch, begin by getting into a deadlift position.

2) Make sure your back is set in a neutral or slightly arched position. 

3) From there, drive up through the hips, putting momentum into the barbell. 

4) Follow through with that by shoulder shrugging and swingi

name: Barbell Preacher Curl
description:
1.Kneel on the floor with your chest supported by a preacher bench, using a wide grip hold an EZ curl bar, palms facing up.

2.Curl the bar upward. Pause.

3.Lower the bar returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Barbell Rear Delt Row
description:
1.With your knees slightly bent and your back straight, bend over and grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip.

2.Keeping your torso still, pull the barbell up towards your chest until your upper arms are parallel to the floor. Pause.

3.Lower the barbell returning to starting position.

4.Rep

name: Barbell Rear Shrug
description:
1) Stand and hold a barbell behind your back with your palms facing backwards. 

2) Begin by raising your shoulders up as far as possible, pause, and then return to the starting position.

name: Barbell Reverse Lunge
description:
1) Position the Barbell across your upper back holding the ends with your hands to keep it stable. 

2) Stand erect with your chest out and your shoulders back, maintaining the natural curve in your back and knees unlocked. Your feet should be about hip-width apart. This is your starting position.

name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
description:
1) Stand straight holding a barbell in overhand grip with feet hip-width apart. Your arms will need to be kept straight for the full exercise. 

2) Start by lowering the bar down by pushing your hips back as your chest and shoulders move ahead of the bar. 

3) Keep lowering until you feel a stre

name: Barbell Seated Shoulder Press
description:
1. When performing this exercise, set up the rack appropriately.

2. Grab the barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing forward).

3. Once you pick up the barbell with the correct grip width, lift the bar up over your head by locking your arms. 

4. Hold at about shoulder level and slightly i

name: Barbell Shrug
description:
1) Stand and hold a barbell with both hands in front of you at arms length, with your hands slightly wider than shoulder width apart. 

2) Begin by raising your shoulders up as far as possible, pause, and then return to the starting position.

name: Barbell Side Bend
description:
1) Stand straight up with a barbell resting on your shoulders and your back straight. 

2) Begin by bending to one side as far as you can and then to the other side as far as you can, using only your waist.

name: Barbell Single Leg Hip Thrust
description:
Sit with your upper back supported on a bench and a barbell across your hips. 

Extend one leg straight out while keeping the other foot flat on the ground. 

Drive through the grounded heel to lift your hips, then lower with control. Alternate sides.

name: barbell situps
description: barbell situps

name: Barbell Skullcrusher
description:
1) Start by pressing up the barbell with your grip less than 12 inches apart. 

2) Slowly lower the barbell towards your head, pause, and raise the barbell again.

name: Barbell Step Up
description:
1. When performing the step up, ensure that your foot, knee and hip are in line while you step up. At the top of the step, ensure that your hips come underneath you.

2. Stand up straight while holding a barbell placed on the back of your shoulders (slightly below the neck) and stand upright behin

name: Barbell Straight Leg Deadlift
description:
1) Start by standing with your feet shoulder width apart. 

2) Pick up the barbell by bending knees to bend over and grasp barbell with a shoulder width overhand grip and back straight. 

3) Lift weight to standing position keeping your back straight. 

4) Begin by bending your hips to lower t

name: Barbell Sumo Deadlift
description:
1.Set your feet much wider than shoulder width. You‚Äôll want to turn them out at about 45 degrees. 

2.During the deadlift, always keep your back set.

3.Take a breath, and then lower your torso pushing your hips back, looking forward with your head with your chest up.

name: Barbell Sumo Deadlift
description:
1.Set your feet much wider than shoulder width. You‚Äôll want to turn them out at about 45 degrees. 

2.During the deadlift, always keep your back set.

3.Take a breath, and then lower your torso pushing your hips back, looking forward with your head with your chest up. 

4.Drive through the flo

name: Barbell Thruster
description:
1) When performing the thruster, you‚Äôll need to be able to clean the barbell up to your chest and shoulders. 

2) From there, you‚Äôll perform a squat. Make sure that you sit back even though you‚Äôre loaded up front with a barbell. 

3) Keep your arms up as high as you can or parallel to the ground

name: Barbell Torso Twist
description:
1.Stand upright holding a PVC pipe or wooden dowel in a wide position grip, placed it onto the back of your neck.

2.Twist your upper torso to one side, keeping your head straight and forward.

3.Twist back to the opposite side, allowing your hips to move.

4.Alternate sides with each rep.

name: Barbell Upright Row
description:
1.Stand upright holding the bar with a closed grip, with your arms straight and palms facing back.

2.Raise the bar up to just below your chin, keeping your elbows above your hands, bar close to your body.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Barbell Wide Grip Bicep Curl
description:
1) Set your knees with a soft bend, hips back and chest up while you perform your wide grip bicep curl. 

2) Keep your elbows tucked in while curling.

name: Barbell Wide Grip Deadlift
description:
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and grip the barbell wider than shoulder width. Lower your hips and keep your back straight. 

Push through your heels to lift the barbell, fully extending your hips and knees at the top. Lower the bar back to the floor in a controlled manner.

name: Barbell Wide Grip Romanian Deadlift
description:
With feet shoulder-width apart, hold the barbell with a wide grip. 

Hinge at the hips to lower the barbell along your legs, keeping your back straight and a slight bend in your knees. 

Stop when you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, then return to standing.

name: Battle Rope Alternating Power Slam
description:
1) When performing this exercise, with a single arm, you will wind up lift the rope high and slam down as hard as you can using your whole body.

description:
1) Set your feet below your hips with a light knee bend. Set your abdominals by drawing them in. Hands holding the battle ropes in the stagecoach grip.

name: Battle Rope Alternating Wave
description:

name: Battle Rope Alternating Wave Get Up
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll alternate the ropes left to right in opposing directions.

name: Battle Rope Alternating Wave Reverse Lunge
description:

3) When doing a reverse lunge, and alternating your feet through them, ensure that yo

name: Battle Rope Alternating Wave Side Lunge

2) At the same time, you‚Äôll also perform a side lunge. When doing a side lunge, you‚Äôll want to ensure that your foot, knee and hip are all in alignment.

name: Battle Rope Alternating Wave Side Shuffle
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll alternate the rope coming up and down between the left and the right side.

2) While also doing repeating squats simultaneously as the same time as the waves.

name: Battle Rope Burpee
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll lift the rope as high as you can before slamming down on to the ground.

2) When you do so, try to slam the ropes down using your abdominal as much as you can.

name: Battle Rope Cross Body Wave
description:

2) When you perform the movement with your arm itself, you‚Äôll be lifting the rope straight up and then slamming it down.

3) Try and not to twist your body while performing the exercise.

4) When you‚Äôre comple

name: Battle Rope Double Wave
description:
1) For this exercise, you‚Äôll want to send waves down on the rope with both arms sending a wave at the same time.

2) If you want to isolate your upper body, you can just use your arms 

3) OR you can make it a full body exercise and include your lower body by pumping through small squats.

name: Battle Rope High Plank Alternating Slams
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll be on top of the rope with your hand hold grip.

2) Maintain a quiet body that means abdominal engaged and hips not rocking back and forth or lifting up too high.

name: Battle Rope In And Out Wave
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll alternate opening your arms as wide as you can.

2) Crossing them slightly in front of you. Then, firing the back out.

3) Each time you come to the center, try to alternate which hand is over top of the other.

name: Battle Rope Inward Circle
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll start at the ground, scoop out around making a circle with your arms and coming in towards the middle.

2) While you‚Äôre doing so, keep yourself in a half squat to ensure a strong foundation.

name: Battle Rope Jumping Jack
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll be doing a jumping jack while holding on to the ropes.

2) When you perform the jumping jack, ensure that the arms come out laterally beside you rather than in front of you.

name: Battle Rope Outward Circle
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll make large circles starting from the middle going outward, scooping up and heading out again.

2) To set your base, get into a half squat and hold yourself there.

name: Battle Rope Overhead Press to Lateral Drop
description:
1. When performing this exercise, it‚Äôll be a combination between a shoulder press and a shoulder fly.

name: Battle Rope Plank Alternating Slams
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll be on top of the rope with your hand hold grip.

2) Maintain a quiet body that means abdominal engaged and hips not rocking back and forth or lifting up too high.

name: Battle Rope Power Slam
description:
1) When performing this exercise, your goal is to send a single wave down the rope.

2) You‚Äôll do so by lifting the rope up high and using your whole body momentum and body weight to slam the rope to the ground.

name: Battle Rope Russian Twist
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll want to ensure that your abdominal are engaged. You do so by shortening the distance between the bottom of your ribs and the top of your hips.

2) As you move the ropes left to right, you‚Äôll want to move from your rib cage to help turn your body.

name: Battle Rope Side Slam
description:
1) When performing this exercise, keeping your hands next to each other, you‚Äôll alternate slamming the ropes to the right and to the left of your body.

2) Bend your torso and allow your legs to help out.

name: Battle Rope Side Slam Alternating Lunge
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll slam the rope alternating from the left to the right side of your body keeping your hands as close together as possible.

2) At the same time, in the best lunge position as possible, alternate and jump back and forth with the rope.

name: Battle Rope Sidewinder
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll want to keep your hands as close together as possible.

2) To perform the movement itself, you‚Äôll want to sway back and forth in your hips as well as rotating in through your torso.

name: Battle Rope Single Arm Elbow Plank Waves
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll want to maintain a quiet body that means abdominals engaged and hips not rocking back and forth.
 
2) Use one arm to send vertical waves down the rope.

3) When you‚Äôve completed one side, do the other.

name: Battle Rope Single Arm Inward Circle
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll scoop along the floor.

2) Make a circle upward towards the air, then bring the arm down and inward.

3) Set a stable base by bending knees and going in to a half squat keeping an isolated or quiet body.

name: Battle Rope Single Arm Outward Circle
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll start at the bottom of the circle. Go up, in across your body an then slam it down out as you complete the circle.

2) Isolate your lower body by going to a half squat and holding yourself there so that all the work is done by the arm on the upper body.

name: Battle Rope Single Arm Wave
description:
1) When performing this exercise, send repetitive waves down the rope just using one arm.

2) You can isolate it to make an upper body exercise OR include repeating squats to make it a full body exercise. 

3) Once you‚Äôre done one side, repeat the number of reps with the other arm.

name: Battle Rope Single Arm Wave Full Plank
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll want to maintain a quiet body that means abdominal engaged and hips not rocking back and forth.

2) When you‚Äôve completed one side, do the other.

name: Battle Rope Single Leg Snake
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll be on one foot. You‚Äôll want to bend into a half squat position.

2) From there, you‚Äôll put outward momentum on the rope as you slide the rope in and out along the floor like snakes.

3) When you‚Äôre done one foot, switch. Then do the other for the same amo

name: Battle Rope Slam Squat Jump
description: 1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll time the power slam of the ropes both happening at the same time, left and right hands with a jump from a squat.

name: Battle Rope Snake
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll want to set your base by getting into half squat position.

2) From there, you put momentum onto the rope outward as you relax and return your arms to center.

3) Push put like a Delt Fly repeatedly.

name: Battle Rope Star Jump
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll essentially do a jumping jack but before you touch down on the ground, your arms and legs will return to center.

2) You‚Äôll want to keep the rope moving up laterally beside you rather than in front of your body.

name: Battle Rope Thruster
description:
1) When performing this exercise, in the overhead position, you want to ensure your biceps are in line with your ears.

2) To make it a little more challenging, you can step back to have a little more resistance from the rope.

3) Keep in mind that you don‚Äôt want to lean back from your back so k

name: Battle Rope Wave Jump Squat
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll move the rope alternating left to right in opposing directions.

2) At the same time, you‚Äôll perform a jump squat.

name: BB Barbell Curtsy Lunge

name: Bear Crawl

(2) From here, keep your body high while staying as squar

3. Slowly return to the start position under control, and repeat for de

name: Bear Plank
description:
1. Start on your hands and knees on the mat.

2. Point your toes into the floor, and lift your knees 1 inch off the floor. 

3. Keep your shoulders directly over top of your hands, hips over knees, and abdominals tight. 

4. Hold this position.

name: Bear Plank with Shoulder Tap
description:
Start on all fours, then lift your knees slightly off the ground so your hips are low and your back is flat, forming a bear plank position. Keep your core engaged and hips stable. 

Lift one hand off the ground and tap the opposite shoulder, then return it to the floor. Alternate sides, tapping ea

name: Bear Position Kettlebell Pull Through
description:
Start in a bear crawl position (hands and feet on the ground, knees bent). 

Reach under your body with one hand to pull the kettlebell across to the opposite side. 

This works the core, shoulders, and coordination.

name: Bear Squat to Downward Dog
description: From a bear squat, straighten your legs and lift your hips into a downward dog position.

name: Bear Squat to Push-Up
description:
Begin in a bear position with hands under shoulders, knees bent at 90 degrees, and hovering slightly above the ground. 

From this position, perform a squat by bringing your hips back slightly while keeping your knees off the ground. 

Return to the bear position and immediately perform a push-u

name: Bear Squat to Spiderman
description: From a bear squat, step one foot forward into a deep lunge, then return and switch sides.

name: Bear Squat to Spinal Wave
description: Perform a bear squat, then transition into a spinal wave by rounding and extending your spine.

name: Bear Stance Single Arm Row
description:
Start in a bear crawl position with your knees slightly off the ground and your arms straight. 

Hold a dumbbell in each hand, and row them towards your torso, keeping your back flat and core tight. 

Lower and repeat.

name: Bear to Stand
description: From a bear crawl position, press through your feet and extend into a standing position.

name: Bear to Step Through
description: From a bear position, step one leg forward and rotate your torso, keeping the movement fluid.

name: Bear to T-Rotation
description: From a bear position, rotate your torso and reach one arm toward the ceiling. Alternate sides.

name: Bear to Underswitch
description: Start in a bear crawl stance and rotate one leg under your body, transitioning to a crab position.

name: Bear Underswitch to Crab Reach
description: Perform a bear underswitch, then reach one arm overhead to extend into a crab reach.

name: Bear Walk Out to Push-Up
description: Start on all fours, then walk your hands forward while keeping your knees slightly off the ground until your body is in a push-up position. Perform a push-up, then walk your hands back toward your feet and return to the starting position. Repeat.

name: Beat Swings
description:
1. Start by gripping a pull up bar with a grip slightly wider than shoulder-width. 

2. Initiate the movement with the shoulders, pushing your body into an arched back position. 

3. From the arched position, lift your hips and legs forwards, driving the body back behind the bar.

4. Continue

name: Ben Bruno Teaches the Landmine Squat
description: Ben Bruno Teaches the Landmine Squat

name: Bench Alternating Leg Raise
description:
Lie flat on a bench with your legs extended off the edge. 

Raise one leg towards the ceiling while keeping the other leg hovering above the bench. 

Lower the raised leg slowly and alternate to the other leg, engaging your core throughout.

name: Bench Bridge March
description:
Start in a hip thrust position on a bench. 

Lift one foot off the ground, bringing the knee towards your chest, then lower it back down. 

Alternate legs in a marching motion, keeping your hips elevated and core engaged.

name: Bench Crunches
description:
Lie on a bench with your feet secured under the foot pads. 

Place your hands behind your head and curl your upper body towards your knees, engaging your abs. 

Lower back down with control to complete the rep.

name: Bench Hip Raise to Hip Lift
description:
Lie on a bench with your upper back and shoulders supported, arms extended behind your head holding onto the bench for stability. 

Keep your legs fully extended and together, hovering slightly above the bench. Engage your core and glutes to raise your hips off the bench (hip raise). 

From this

name: Bench Hip Thrust
description:
Sit on the ground with your upper back against a bench, feet flat on the floor. 

Place a weight on your hips, then thrust your hips upward until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees. 

Lower back down, focusing on your glutes.

name: Bench Hopover Burpee
description:
Start standing on one side of a bench or elevated platform. Jump laterally over the bench, landing softly on the other side. 

Immediately drop into a burpee by placing your hands on the floor, kicking your feet back into a plank, performing a push-up, then jumping your feet forward and explosivel

name: Bench Jumping Step Ups
description:
Stand facing a bench and step one foot onto the bench. 

Explode upwards, driving the opposite knee up while switching feet in mid-air. 

Land with the opposite foot on the bench and repeat for a high-intensity cardio and lower body exercise.

name: Bench Knee Tuck to V Up
description:
Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands placed slightly behind your hips on the sides of the bench for support. 

Lean your torso back slightly and lift your feet off the bench with your knees bent. Begin by pulling your knees toward your chest (knee tuck), engaging your core. 

Then, extend

name: Bench Leg Raise
description:
1. When performing this exercise, you'll want to ensure that your lower back keeps constant pressure into the bench.

2. You do so by keeping your abdominals engaged as your legs come back down towards the beginning position.

3. Lie with your back flat on a bench and your legs extended in off t

name: Bench lower leg lifts
description: Try to keep back as flat as you can.

description:
Start in a plank position with your hands on a bench, shoulders stacked over your hands, and feet extended behind you.

name: Bench Plyo Push Ups
description:
Place your hands on a bench, slightly wider than shoulder-width, and extend your legs behind you so your body is in a plank position. 

Lower your chest toward the bench, then explosively push through your hands to lift your upper body off the bench slightly. 

Land softly back on the bench and

name: Bench Push Up to Mountain Climber
description:
Place your hands on a bench, slightly wider than shoulder-width, and extend your legs behind you so your body forms a plank. 

Perform a push-up by lowering your chest toward the bench, then pressing back up to full arm extension. Immediately bring one knee toward your chest, then switch legs quic

name: Bench Reverse Crunch
description:
Lie on a bench with your knees bent and feet off the edge. Use your core to lift your hips off the bench, curling your knees towards your chest. 

Slowly lower your hips back down, maintaining control to work your lower abs.

name: Bench Side Plank Hip Dip
description:
Get into a side plank position on a bench with your bottom forearm supporting your upper body and your bottom leg extended. 

Stack your top leg over the bottom for balance and place your top hand on your hip. Keep your core engaged and slowly lower your top hip toward the bench in a controlled mo

name: Bench Side Plank Oblique Crunch
description:
Get into a side plank position on a bench with your bottom forearm supporting your upper body and your bottom leg extended. 

Extend your top arm overhead and stack your top leg over the bottom for balance. Engage your core and, in a controlled motion, crunch by bringing your top hand toward your

name: Bench Side Plank Row to Press
description:
Get into a side plank position with your forearm or hand on a bench, keeping your body in a straight line from head to feet. 

Hold a dumbbell in your top hand with your arm fully extended toward the floor. Engage your core and pull the dumbbell up toward your ribcage (row), keeping your elbow clo

name: Bench Single Leg Hip Thrust
description:
Similar to the Bench Hip Thrust, but perform the movement with one leg extended out. 

This increases the challenge on the glutes and hamstrings of the working leg while testing your balance and stability.

Stand facing the side of a bench with one foot on the bench and one foot on the ground. 

Push off the bench with the raised foot, switching feet in mid-air to land with the opposite foot on the bench, mimicking a skating motion.

description:
Sit on the edge of a bench, leaning back slightly with your hands gripping the sides. 

Lift your legs off the ground and alternate tapping your toes on the bench, keeping your core engaged to maintain balance.

description:
1) When performing dip on the bench, keep your elbows pointing straight behind you. 

2) Keep your body as close to the bench as possible as you dip down.

name: Bench Twist Crunches
description:
Perform a standard bench crunch but alternate bringing one shoulder towards the opposite knee. 

This targets the obliques as well as the upper abs, enhancing rotational strength.

name: Bench Up & Overs
description:
Stand beside a bench with one foot on the bench. 

Push through the foot to step over the bench, landing with the opposite foot on the bench as you bring your other foot down on the other side. 

Repeat in a continuous motion for a cardio and lower body workout.

description:

name: Bench V-sits
description:
Sit on the edge of a bench, balancing on your glutes. 

Extend your legs and lean back slightly, then pull your knees towards your chest while bringing your upper body forward. 

Extend back to the starting position, maintaining core tension.

name: Bent Knee Inverted Rows
description:
1. Start with your feet in front of the bar, heels on the floor and body in a straight line.

name: Bent Over Medicine Ball High Row
description:
Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a medicine ball with both hands. Hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back flat and core engaged, with the ball hanging just below your chest. 

Pull the ball explosively toward your upper chest or collarbone, driving your elbows outward and upward.

name: Bicycle Crunch
description:
1) For the bicycle crunch, sit on your pelvis or your sit bones with your feet off the ground and upper body tall. 

2) Bring opposite elbow to the opposite knee. 

3) Twist between your pelvis and your rib cage.

name: Bird Dog
description:
1) When lifting your leg in this exercise, try to isolate the movement from your hip or your glute so squeeze your bum. 

2) Get into a 4-point position.

name: Bird Dog Balance Row
description:
Start on all fours with a dumbbell in one hand. Extend the opposite arm and leg out while keeping your back flat. 

Row the dumbbell with your free hand, and then return to starting position. 

Alternate sides.

name: Body Weight Alternating Forward Lunge
description:
1) When performing this exercise, step out far enough so you can drop out to 90 degrees throughout your lower body segments. Keeping your upper body over top of the back knee.

name: Body Weight Calf Raise
description:

2) Roll all the way up to your toes.

name: Body Weight Forward Lunge
description:
1) As you step forward, plant your foot, bend your knees so that you reach 90 degrees. 

2) Be careful that your front knee doesn‚Äôt lunge forward. Keep back tall over top of the back knee. 

3) Return to start position. 

4) Repeat.

name: Body Weight Reverse Lunge

1) As you step back, plant your foot and put your body weight over top of your back knee. 

2) Keep your body position tall. All lower body segments in 90 degrees. 

3) Return to start position. 

4) Repeat.

name: Body Weight Single Leg Deadlift
description:
1)  When performing this exercise, ensure that your upper body maintains a safe position for your back. Chest up, a slight arch or neutral spine.  

2) Stand on one leg and tip at the hips sitting back into your heel on your standing leg. 

3) Squeeze your glutes and stand. 

4) Repeat.

name: Body Weight Squat
description:
1)  When performing the squat, let your body weight shift back into your heels as you sit your hips back.

name: Bodyweight Alternating Cossack Squat
description:
Perform a side-to-side squat, alternating between each leg. Lower your body toward one leg, keeping the other leg straight, then return to the starting position and switch sides. 

This enhances hip mobility, strength, and flexibility.

name: Bodyweight Alternating Step Up
description:
Perform a step up, alternating legs with each repetition. 

This variation increases coordination and balance, engaging your quads, glutes, and calves.

name: Bodyweight Arms Overhead Squat
description: Perform a squat while keeping both arms extended overhead, ensuring a neutral spine and deep squat depth.

name: Bodyweight A-skip
description: Perform a skipping motion, alternating knees up toward the chest while driving the opposite arm forward.

name: Bodyweight Bear Squat To Plank
description:
Start in bear position (on hands and knees, hips lifted). Squat back by lowering your hips and sitting into a squat, then transition into a plank position by straightening your legs and bringing your body into a straight line. 

Targets core, shoulders, and legs.

name: Bodyweight Bent Knee Calf Raise
description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent. Lift your heels off the ground by pushing through the balls of your feet, engaging your calves. Slowly lower back down. 

Strengthens calves and ankles.

name: Bodyweight Bent Knee Single Leg Calf Raise
description:
Stand on one leg with your knee slightly bent. Raise your heel off the ground by pushing through the ball of your foot.

Lower slowly. Focuses on calf strength, balance, and stability.

name: Bodyweight B-skip
description:
Perform a skipping movement by lifting one knee to waist height, then extending your leg straight out in front of you while hopping on the other foot. 

Alternate legs with each skip. This improves hip flexor and hamstring mobility.

name: Bodyweight Cossack Squat
description:

description:
Start seated with your hands behind you, knees bent, and feet flat on the floor.

Lift your hips off the ground and rotate one arm overhead while keeping your hips elevated. This engages your chest, shoulders, and core.

name: Bodyweight Deadbug
description:
Lie on your back with arms extended towards the ceiling and knees bent. 

Lower opposite arm and leg towards the floor, then return to start. Alternate sides.

name: Bodyweight Deadlift
description: Perform a hinge movement by pushing your hips back, lowering your torso while maintaining a flat back, then standing up again.

name: Bodyweight Kang Squat
description:
Start standing, hinge forward at the hips into a good-morning position, then drop into a squat. 

Reverse the movement to return to standing.

name: Bodyweight Lateral Squat
description: Step laterally into a squat position, bending one knee while keeping the other leg straight, and return to center.

name: Bodyweight Rear Foot Elevated Squat
description:
Place one foot on a bench or elevated surface behind you. Lower your hips and bend the front knee to perform a squat, keeping your chest lifted. 

This targets quads, glutes, and improves balance.

name: Bodyweight Reverse Plank
description: Sit with legs extended, then lift your hips off the ground into a reverse plank position, holding for time while engaging the core.

name: Bodyweight Single Leg Calf Raise
description:
Stand on one leg and slowly raise your heel off the ground. Focus on pushing through the ball of your foot. 

Strengthens calves and improves balance.

name: Bodyweight Single Side Step Up
description:
Stand in front of a bench or sturdy platform. 

Step one foot onto the platform and drive through your heel to lift your body up, bringing the opposite knee towards your chest. 

Step back down and alternate legs for a functional lower body workout.

name: Bodyweight Snap Down
description: From a standing position, rise up onto your toes with arms overhead, then quickly drop into an athletic stance by bending your knees and swinging your arms back, landing softly. Reset and repeat the snapping motion continuously.

name: Bodyweight Spiderman Lunge To Rotation
description:
Start in a plank position. Bring one foot toward your hand in a lunge position, then rotate your torso to reach your opposite arm towards the sky. 

This engages the core, shoulders, and hips.

name: Bodyweight Split Squat
description:
1)  While performing this exercise, ensure that your body is over top of your back leg. 

2) Keep all your lower body segments at 90 degrees. 

3) Extend up into a standing position. 

4) Repeat.

name: Bodyweight Squat To Hinge
description:
Start in a deep squat position, then straighten your legs and shift your hips back into a hinge position. 

Engage your hamstrings and lower back. Focuses on leg mobility and core stability.

name: Bodyweight Sumo Squat
description:
Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and toes pointed outward. 

Lower into a squat, keeping your knees in line with your toes. 

This targets the quads, glutes, and inner thighs.

name: Bodyweight Walking Lunge
description:
Perform a lunge while walking forward, alternating legs with each step. 

Focus on a deep lunge and proper posture.

description:
