2.Jump your feet in, bringing your knees to your chest while keeping your hands on the floor.

3.Come to an upright position and jump into the air, raising your arms overhead.
 
4.Return to the mid position and then jump your feet back out to a straig

name: Burpee Broad Jump
description: Perform a burpee, then explode into a forward broad jump before resetting.

name: Burpee Pull Ups
description:
1. Place your hands on the floor, jumping feet backwards, and lower your chest to the floor. 

2. Push yourself back up to a standing position, then jump upwards to grab a pull up bar. 

3. Immediately pull yourself upwards until your chin is above the bar. Lower yourself and repeat.

name: Burpee to Target
description:
1. Place your hands on the floor, jumping feet backwards, and lowering your chest to the floor. 

2. Push yourself back up to a standing position, then jump upwards to touch an overhead target with both of your hands.

name: Burpee to Tuck Jump
description:
Start standing tall with your feet shoulder-width apart and your hands at your sides. Begin the movement by explosively jumping upward with your hands reaching overhead. 

Upon landing, immediately lower into a squat, place your hands on the floor, and jump your feet back into a high plank positio

name: Butt Kickers
description:
1. Stand with your legs shoulder-width apart. Your arms should be bent.

2. Flex the right knee and kick your right heel up toward your glutes. Bring the right foot back down.

3. Try to keep your knees in line with your body as best as you can.

4. As the right leg comes down, flex your left

name: Butterfly Pull Up
description:
1. Start by gripping a pull up bar with a grip slightly wider than shoulder-width. 

2. Initiate the movement with the shoulders, pushing your body into an arched back position. 

3. From the arched position, lift your hips and legs straight up towards the bar, pulling yourself upwards at the sa

name: BW circuit push-up/alt knee / toe tap
Cable Bent Over Chest Fly
description:
1.Bend over at the waist holding handles with your arms straight out to your sides at shoulder height and your feet split.

2.Pull the handles down together in front until they meet, keeping your arms straight.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Bent Over Row
description:
1.Bend forward at your waist with your knees slightly bent and your back flat while holding the handles with your arms straight.

2.Pull the handles towards your chest.

3.Lower the handles back to a straight-arm position, keeping your back flat throughout.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Bicep Curl
description:
1.Stand facing the low pulley and attach a short bar to the low cable. Grasp the bar with your palms up and stand with your arms straight.

2.Bending on the elbows, curl the bar up towards your shoulders. Pause

3.Lower the bar returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Curl and Press
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handles at your sides with your arms straight.

2.Raise the handles up to your shoulders, bending at your elbows. Do not sway back and forth as you lift and lower the handles.

3.Lower the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Curtsy Lunge
description: Cable Curtsy Lunge

name: Cable External Rotation
description:
1.Stand upright holding a cable in one hand across your mid-section with your elbow bent at 90 degrees and your upper arm against your body. 

2.Pull the cable out and away from your body, keeping your elbow at 90 degrees and your upper arm against your side.

3.Return to starting position.

4

name: Cable Flat Chest Fly
description:
1.Lie on a flat bench holding the handles, arms extended.

2.Raise the handles above your chest, elbows slightly bent.

3.Lower down the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Front Raise
description:
1. Standing upright, grabbing the straight bar with both hands.

2.  Raise arm fully extended. Raise arm until hands are at eye level. Ensure core is tight while raising arm. 

3. Lower to starting position. Repeat.

name: Cable Full Kneeling Pallof Press
description: Kneel on both knees with cable at chest. Press forward with both hands, maintaining core stability to resist twisting.

name: Cable Glute Crossover Kickback
description: Step over the cable with the working leg behind. Keep the leg straight and extend it back in a diagonal path. Pause at the top and return.

name: Cable Hamstring RDL
description: Cable Hamstring RDL

name: Cable High Bicep Curl
description:
1) Attach the stirrup attachment to two overhead cable pulleys. 

2) Stand in the middle of the weight stack and grasp both handles with an underhand grip. 

3) Your arms should stretch straight out to your sides. 

4) Keeping your elbows still, curl your hands towards your shoulders, pause, a

name: Cable High-Low Chop
description:
1.Stand upright facing to one side holding a handle in both hands with your arms straight over one shoulder.

2.Pull the cable down from your shoulder to your opposite hip.

3.Concentrate on moving your hips and shoulders, not your arms.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the

name: Cable Hip Abduction
description:
1.Stand to the side of a cable system with the cable attached to the ankle of your outer leg.

2.Draw this leg out and away from your body. Keep both legs straight and maintain your balance by holding onto the system if necessary.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one sid

name: Cable Incline Bench Seated Tricep Extension
description:
1.Lie on an incline bench facing away from a cable system, holding a rope at head height with your elbows bent.

2.Straighten your arms out in front, keeping your shoulders steady.

3.Bend at the elbows, returning the cable to the start position.

name: Cable Incline Chest Fly
description:
1.Lie on an incline bench set at 45 degrees holding the handles on your side, elbows slightly bent.

2.Raise the handles up over your chest.

3.Lower the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Internal Shoulder Rotation
description:
1.Stand upright holding a cable out to the side with your elbow at 90 degrees and your upper arm against your side.

2.Pull the cable across your mid-section, keeping your elbow at 90 degrees and your upper arm against your side.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side

name: Cable Iron Cross Pulldown
description:
1.Stand holding handles with your arms straight out to sides at shoulder height.

2.Pull the handles down to your sides, keep your arms straight.

3.Raise the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Iso Half Kneeling Pallof Press
description: In a half kneeling stance, hold the cable straight out in front of your chest and maintain tension without moving. Engage your core.

name: Cable Kayak Row Variation
description: Cable Kayak Row Variation

name: Cable Kneeling Alternating Twisting Crunch
description:
1.Kneel holding a rope cable in front of you, elbows slightly bent.

2.Curl your head and torso down to one side towards your knees, keeping your hands at the sides of your head.

3.Return upright returning to starting position.

4.Repeat and alternate.

name: Cable Kneeling Crunch
description:
1.Kneel and grasp a cable rope attached to a high pulley, arms extended.

2.Curl your head and torso down to your knees, hands at the sides of your head.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Kneeling High-Low Chop
description:
1.Kneel holding a rope in both hands with your arms straight over one shoulder.

2.Pull the cable down from your shoulder to your opposite hip. Concentrate on moving your hips and shoulders, not your arms.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Kneeling Low-High Chop
description:
1.Hold a rope in both hands with your arms straight to the side of one hip, kneeling on the inside knee, arms straight, keeping the shoulders square.

2.Pull the cable up from the side of your hip to over your opposite shoulder, twisting your torso and keeping your arms straight. Concentrate on mo

name: Cable Kneeling Overhead Tricep Extension
description:
1.Kneel facing away from a cable system holding the handles in each hand at the back of your head, elbows bent.

2.Straighten your arms overhead, keeping your shoulders steady.

3.Bend at the elbows returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Kneeling Rotational Row
description:
1.Kneel on one knee holding a handle in the hand on the same side with your arm straight and your back flat.

2.Pull the handle straight into your chest, elbows back and hand towards the shoulder.

3.Return to starting position by lowering the handle back to a straight-arm position.

4.Complet

name: Cable Kneeling Row
description: Kneel facing the cable machine. Grab the handle with both hands and pull toward your torso, keeping elbows close to your body. Squeeze your back.

name: Cable Kneeling Single Arm Lat Pulldown
description: Kneel facing the cable machine. Pull the handle down with one arm from overhead to your side, keeping elbow tucked. Engage your lats.

name: Cable Kneeling Single Leg Donkey Kickback
description: Kneel on all fours with the cable attached to one ankle. Drive that leg up and back, keeping the knee bent at 90¬į. Squeeze the glute, then return.

name: Cable Lateral Raise
description:
1.Stand upright holding the cables with your hands crossed and your feet hip-width apart. 

2.Raise the handles up and outward to shoulder height, keeping your arms straight or with a slight bend at the elbows.

3.Lower the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Low to High Alternating Cross Over
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handles at your sides, palms facing out.

2.Using your chest muscles, bring your hands up with one hand higher than the other, elbows slightly bent.

3.Lower the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat and alternate higher and lower hand.

name: Cable Low-High Chop
description:
"1.Stand upright facing to one side, holding a handle in both hands at hip level with your arms straight.

2.Pull the cable up from your hip to over your opposite shoulder. Move through your hips and shoulders, not your arms.

3.Lower the handle returning to starting position.

4.Complete all

name: Cable Lunge and Crunch
description:
1.Stand upright holding a rope cable behind your neck.

2.Take a step forward, dropping your back knee to the floor and curl your head and torso down to your midsection, keeping your hands at the sides of your head.

3.Push off front foot to return back to the upright position.

4.Complete all

name: Cable Lying Down Bicep Curl
description:
1.Lie down, with body flat on the floor holding a rope in both hands, arms straight.

2.Curl the rope towards your chest.

3.Squeeze the biceps and then slowly lower the rope back to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Lying Down Rope Bicep Curl
description: Lie down facing up with the rope attachment in hand. Curl the rope toward your shoulders while keeping upper arms flat on the floor.

name: Cable Lying Down Torso Twist
description:
1.Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat, holding the rope on one side.

2.Twisting at your torso, roll your hands over the other side.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Lyng Down Single Leg Curl
description: Lie face down with a cable strap on one ankle. Curl the leg toward your glutes, keeping hips down. Return with control.

name: Cable Pallof Press
description: Kneel with one knee down, holding the cable at chest height. Press straight out, resisting rotation. Hold briefly, then return.

name: Cable Pallof Press With Overhead Raise
description: From standing, press the cable out from your chest, then lift overhead in a straight line. Return in reverse. Stay square and stable.

name: Cable Palms Up Rear Delt Crossover
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handles with your arms extending away from the body at shoulder level, palms up.

2.Extend your arms to the side, straight out at both sides.

3.Return your arms back to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Pull Through
description:
1.Squat with your back to a cable system holding a rope between your legs with your arms straight and back flat.

2.Stand upright, pulling the rope forward and up between your legs, keeping your arms straight.

3. Return to starting position.

name: Cable Pullover
description:
1) Lie with your head and back resting on a bench, hold the handles with elbows straight and arms fully extended overhead.

2) Pull the rope forward straight up over your chest, keeping your arms straight.

3) Lower the rope back to starting position.

name: Cable Push Pull
description:
1.Stand upright holding one handle with your arm straight out in front and the other at your shoulder with your elbow bent, feet split front to back.

2.Press one handle out in front as you pull the other into your chest. Keep your hands at shoulder height. 

3. Return to starting position.

4

name: Cable Resisted Crunch
description:
1.Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat, place your hands by your sides, hands holding rope above the head.

2.Crunch by lifting your head and shoulders off the floor, keeping your feet flat.

3.Lower your head and shoulders returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Reverse Curl
description:
1.Stand upright, grasp the cable bar attached to a low pulley, shoulder width overhand grip. 

2.With the elbows to the side, raise the bar until forearms are vertical. Pause. 

3.Lower until the arms are fully extended returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Reverse Grip Drag Curl
description: Stand tall and hold cable handles at thighs. Curl while dragging elbows back behind the torso. Focus on biceps contraction.

name: Cable Reverse Grip Tricep Pulldown
description:
1.For this exercise, when you set up the machine, ensure that the rollers are above your head. 

2.Secondly, you wanna soft knee bend and to sit your hips back. 

3.Also, remember try not to let the weights tap down in between every single repetition.

4. Grasp a straight bar with a palm up gr

name: Cable Reverse Lunges
description: Cable Reverse Lunges

name: Cable rope bent over tricep ext
Cable Rope Curl
description:
1.Stand upright, grasp the rope.

2.With the elbows to the side, raise the rope until forearms are vertical. Pause. 

3.Lower until the arms are fully extended returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Rope Face Pull
description:
1.Stand upright holding a rope with your palms facing each other arms straight at shoulder height and your back flat.

2.Pull the rope straight in toward your face, bending elbows up and high, full extension.

3.Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth.

name: Cable Rope Hammer Curl
description:
1) Attach a rope attachment to a low pulley. Stand face forwards and about 12 inches away from the machine. 

2) Grasp the rope with a palms-in grip and stand straight up. 

3) Put your elbows at your side and KEEP them there during the entire movement. 

4) Your elbows should not move. Pull y

name: Cable Rope Lying Down Curl
description:
1.Lie on your back while holding the handles, feet on the platform.

2.Slowly curl the handles up toward your chest as you breathe out and you squeeze the biceps.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Rope Overhead Tricep Extension
description:
1.Stand upright facing away from a cable system holding the rope with both arms behind your head with your elbows bent. 

2. Make sure core is flexed, no arching. Extend your arms overhead, keeping your shoulders steady and core tight. 

3. Return to starting position. Repeat.

name: Cable Rope Seated High Row
description:
1.Sit upright holding the ends of the rope.

2.Pull the rope toward your neck, bending at the elbows.

3.Release the rope returning to starting to position. Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Rope Skullcrusher
description:
1.Lie on a bench holding a rope in both hands behind your head with your elbows bent. 

2.Straighten your arms over your head, keeping your shoulders steady.

3. Return to starting position. 

4. Repeat. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

name: Cable Rope Squat and Row
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handle with your arms straight out in front, your knees slightly bent and your back flat.

2.Pull the handle straight in to your chest. Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth.

3.Return to starting position. 

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Rope Tricep Extension
description:
1. Grasp a rope attached to a high cable pulley. 

2.Your forearms should be parallel to the floor. With your elbows against your sides, push the rope downwards as far as possible. 

3. Pause and return to starting position.

name: Cable Seated Chest Fly
description:
1.Sit upright holding the handles with your arms straight out to the side at shoulder height, palms facing away the body.

2.Pull the handles together across in front until they meet at chest height, keeping your arms straight.

3.Extend arms on side returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Seated Close Grip Row
description:
1.Sit upright holding the handle with your arms straight out in front and your back flat.

2.Pull the handle straight in to your chest, bending at the elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together.

3.Release the handle returning to starting position. Remain upright throughout and do not sw

name: Cable Seated Close Row
description:
1.Sit on a bench holding the handle with both arms straight out in front. This will be your starting point.

2.Pull the handles at the same time into your chest.

3. Return to starting position. 

4. Repeat.

name: Cable Seated Curl
description:
1.Sit upright holding the handles with your arms straight down by your sides.

2.Raise the handles up to your shoulders, bending at the elbows.

3.Lower to starting position and repeat.

name: Cable Seated Lower Chest Fly
description:
1.Sit upright holding the handles with your arms straight down by your sides, palms facing outwards.

2.Raise the handles together until they meet at chest height, keeping your arms straight.

3.Lower arms returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Seated Rotational Row
description:
1.Sit holding the handle in one hand with your arm straight out in front, your palm facing in and your back flat.

2.Pull the handle straight in to your chest and twist your torso to this side, bringing your elbow back behind as far as possible. Remain upright throughout.

3. Return to starting

name: Cable Seated Row
description:
1) Sit at a seated cable row station with feet against the pads and knees slightly bent. 

2) Lean forward and grasp the pulley handly with both hands and arms fully extended. 

3) With your back straight, pull the handles towards your stomach, pause, and return to the starting position.

name: Cable Seated Single Arm Close Row
description:
Sit on a cable row bench with feet firmly planted and knees slightly bent

Grab the close-grip handle with one hand

Keep your spine straight, chest lifted, and core engaged

Pull the handle toward your torso, driving your elbow straight back

Squeeze your shoulder blade toward your spine at

name: Cable Seated Single Arm Row
description:
1.Sit upright holding the handle in one hand with your arm straight out in front.

2.Pull the handle straight in to your chest.

3.Release the handle returning to starting position. Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to t

name: Cable Seated Wide Grip Row
description:
1.Sit upright holding the handle with a wide grip, arms straight in front.

2.Pull the handle toward you, bending at the elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together.

3.Release the handle returning to starting position. Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth.

4.Repeat

name: Cable Seated Wide Row
description:
1.Sit upright holding the handle with your arms straight out in front and your back flat.

2.Pull the handle straight in to your chest, bending at the elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together.

3.Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth.

name: Cable Shoulder Press
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handles at shoulder height with your elbows bent.

2.Press the handles overhead, fully extending your arms.

3.Lower the handles down returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Shrug
description:
1) Stand upright facing a cable system, holding the handle with palms facing down, arms straight.

2) Shrug your shoulders, raising them as high as possible, keeping your arms straight. Pause.

3) Lower your shoulders returning to starting position.

4) Repeat.

name: Cable Side Bend
description:
1.Stand holding a cable handle on one side, bent over slightly to that side.

2.Bend your torso to the opposite side, moving through your midsection.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

name: Cable Single Arm Bent Over Reverse Fly
description:
1.Bend over at the waist, straight back, one hand holding the handle.

2.Raise the handle extending it away from the body, arm straight.

3.Lower the handle returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Single Arm Bicep Curl
description:
1) Stand upright, one hand holding the handle, arm straight, palm facing up.

2) Curl the handle toward your shoulder, elbows bent.

3) Lower the handle returning to starting position.

4) Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

name: Cable Single Arm Chest Press
description: Stand in a staggered stance with cable behind you. Press the handle forward with one arm, keeping core tight. Return slowly.

name: Cable Single Arm Front Raise
description:
1) Stand upright holding a handle at your side with your arm straight.

2) Raise the handle up in front to shoulder height, keeping your arm straight.

3) Lower back to the start position and repeat, keeping your back flat and arm straight throughout. 

4) Complete all reps on one side before

name: Cable Single Arm Kneeling Press
description:
1.Kneel on one knee facing away from a cable system, holding the rope in the hand on the same side, elbow bent.

2.Press forward, arm straight.

3.Bend at the elbows returning to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

name: Cable Single Arm Lateral Raise
description: Stand beside the cable machine. With the cable in one hand and arm slightly bent, raise the arm out to the side to shoulder height. Lower with control.

name: Cable Single Arm Preacher Curl
description:
1) When performing this exercise, set the rollers near the bottom of the machine.

2) Also, make sure that you‚Äôre not setting down the weights in between every single repetition. 

3) Grab the bar and fully extend your arms on top of the preacher bench pad. This will be your starting position.

name: Cable Single Arm Rear Delt Fly
description:
1.Bend over at the waist holding the handles with your hands crossed, your arms straight and your feet hip-width apart.

2.Raise the handles up and outward to shoulder height, keeping your back flat.

3.Lower the handles back to starting position, bending at the elbows if necessary.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Single Arm Reverse Fly
description: Stand bent forward with cable in one hand. Sweep the arm out and up to shoulder height. Squeeze the rear deltoid and return.

name: Cable Single Arm Shoulder Press
description:
1.Stand upright holding one handle at shoulder height with your elbow bent, palms away from the body.

2.Press the handle overhead, extending your arm fully. Keep your back flat and remain upright throughout the movement.

3.Lower the handle returning to starting position.

4.Complete all reps

name: Cable Single Arm Standing Fly
description:
1.Stand upright holding one handle with your arm straight out to the side just below shoulder height, palms facing away from the body.

2.Pull the handle across in front to the middle at chest height, keeping your arm straight.

3.Bring the handle back to starting position.

4.Complete all rep

name: Cable Single Arm Standing Overhead Tricep Extension
description:
1.Stand upright facing away from a cable system holding a handle with one arm behind your head with your elbows bent. 

2. Make sure core is flexed, no arching. Extend your arms overhead, keeping your shoulders steady and core tight. 

3. Return to starting position.  Repeat with opposite hand

name: Cable Single Arm Tricep Kickback
description: Lean slightly forward, elbow at your side. Extend your arm back until straight, squeezing the tricep. Return slowly.

name: Cable Single Arm Tricep Pushdown
description:
1) With an underhand grip, grasp a stirrup handle attached to the high-cable pulley and face your body towards the weight stack. 

2) Your starting position should be where your arm forms a 90 degree angle. 

3) Using your triceps, pull the stirrup handle down until your arm is straight, pause,

name: Cable Single Leg Hip Abduction
description: Stand sideways to the machine with the cable on the outside ankle. Lift the leg away from your body, keeping it straight. Pause, then return.

name: Cable Single Leg Hip Extension
description: Stand facing forward with cable on one ankle. Drive the leg back behind you while keeping it straight and torso tall. Engage your glutes.

name: Cable Single Leg Kickback
description:
1) Attach your ankle to an ankle cuff that is hooked to a low cable pulley. Face the weight stack.

2) Pull your leg back in an arc-like motion.

3) At full extension, squeeze your glutes and then bring your leg back down to starting position.

4) Complete all reps on one side before switching

name: Cable Single Leg Step Up
description: Cable Single Leg Step Up

name: Cable SL glute med step over
Cable Split Stance Pallof Press
description: In a staggered stance, hold the cable at your chest. Press it straight out and resist rotation through your torso. Bring it back with control.

name: Cable Standing Clamshell
description: Stand with knees slightly bent and the cable on the outside ankle. Rotate your knee outward while keeping feet close. Squeeze glutes and return.

name: Cable Standing Close Grip Row
description: Using both hands, pull the cable toward your torso with elbows tight to your body. Engage the lats and return slowly.

name: Cable Standing Close Row
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handle with your arms straight out in front, your knees slightly bent and your back flat.

2.Pull the handle straight in to your chest. Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth.

3.Return to starting position. 

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Standing Crossover Chest Fly
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handles with your arms straight and extended out to the side at shoulder height, palms facing away the body.

2.Pull the handles together, hands crossover in the middle, in front of your chest. Try to keep your arms straight throughout or use a slight bend at the elbows

name: Cable Standing High Curl
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handles out to each side at shoulder height with your arms straight, palms up.

2.Pull the handles into your shoulders, bending at your elbows. Keep your elbows and upper arms at shoulder height throughout.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Standing High To Low Fly
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handles out to each side at shoulder height, elbows bent.

2.Pull the handles down, hitting the lower chest.

3.Raise the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Standing High To Low Row
description: With the cable high, pull diagonally down across the body to the opposite hip. Keep elbow high and core engaged.

name: Cable Standing Horizontal Chest Fly
description:
"1.Stand upright holding the handles with your arms straight out to the sides just below shoulder height, palms away from the body.

2.Pull the handles together across in front until they meet at chest height, elbows bent.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat."

name: Cable Standing Knee Drive
description: With the cable on one ankle, drive your knee up toward your chest in a controlled motion. Maintain upright posture and engage your core.

name: Cable Standing Low To High Fly
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handles out to each side with your arms straight, palms facing away the body.

2.Raise the handles together until they meet at chest height, keeping your arms straight.

3.Lower the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Standing Rear Delt Crossover
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handles in front of your chest, arms straight.

2.Extend arms to the side, keeping them straight.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable Standing Single Arm Close Row
description:
1.Stand holding the handle in one hand with your arm straight out in front, your palm facing in and your back flat.

2.Pull the handle straight in to your chest.

3.Return to starting position. Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth or twist at your wrist.

4.Complete all rep

name: Cable Standing Single Arm Row
description: Stand with one foot forward. With the cable in one hand, row the handle toward your waist, keeping the elbow close. Squeeze your back.

name: Cable Standing Single Arm Wide Grip Row
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handle in one hand with your arm straight out in front, your knees slightly bent and your back flat.

2.Pull the handle straight in to your chest. Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth.

3. Return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one

name: Cable Standing Single Leg Kickback
description: With the cable attached to one ankle, bend your knee and drive your heel back and up. Keep core engaged and avoid arching the back.

name: Cable Standing Upright Row
description:
1) Stand and grasp a straight bar connected to the low pulley of a cable machine with your hands shoulder width apart and arms hanging. 

2) Pull the bar straight up till just below your chin and your elbows out. 

3) Pause at the top, and return to starting position.

name: Cable Standing Wide Grip Row
description:
1.Hold the bar using a wide grip with your arms straight out in front, your knees slightly bent and your back flat.

2.Pull the bar straight into your chest, bending at the elbows. Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth.

3.Repeat.

name: Cable Straight Arm Lat Pulldown
description: Stand tall with cable overhead. Pull the straight arms down to your sides while keeping shoulders down. Focus on lats.

name: Cable Straight Arm Rope Lat Pulldown
description: With rope attached overhead, pull straight arms down toward hips. Squeeze lats and control the return.

name: Cable Straight Bar Skullcrusher
description:
1.Lie on a bench holding the straight bar behind your head with your elbows bent. 

2.Straighten your arm over your head, keeping your shoulder steady.

3. Return to starting position. 

4. Repeat. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

name: Cable Straight Bar Tricep Pushdown
description:
1) Grip the bar in both hands at chest level with your elbows bent. 

2) Push the bar down, fully extending your arms and keeping your shoulders steady. 

3) Your hands should finish at the top of your thighs in the downward position.

name: Cable Tricep Kickback
description:
1.Grasp the cable with one hand, sit back (like sitting in a chair), lean forward

2.Using your lat, pull cable onto your side, elbows tight towards hips.

3.Extend the tricep all the way, arm straight. Keep both legs straight and maintain your balance.

4.Repeat.Complete all reps on one side

name: Cable Underhand Pulldown
description:
1) Grasp a cable bar from a high pulley with an underhand grip and your arms and shoulders extended. 

2) Pull down the cable bar until it reaches your upper chest, pause and return to start position.

name: Cable Upper Crossover
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handles at shoulder level, arms straight, palms facing down.

2.Using your chest muscles, bring your hands down with one hand lower than the other, elbows slightly bent.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat and alternate higher and lower hand.

name: Cable Upright Row
description:
1.Stand upright holding a rope in both hands with your arms straight out in front.

2.Pull the rope straight up to your neck, bending at the elbows. Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth.

3.Return to starting position.

4. Repeat.

name: Cable V Bar Tricep Pushdown
description:
1.Grip the bar in both hands at chest level with your elbows bent.

2.Push the bar down, fully extending your arms and keeping your shoulders steady.

3.Your hands should finish at the top of your thighs in the downward position.

4. Return to starting position.

5. Repeat.

name: Cable V-Bar Overhead Tricep Extension
description:
1) Stand upright facing away from a cable system holding the bar with both arms behind your head with your elbows bent. 

2) Make sure core is flexed, no arching. Extend your arms overhead, keeping your shoulders steady and core tight. 

3) Return to starting position. Repeat.

name: Cable wide bicep curl
description:
1.Stand upright holding the handles out to each side with your arms straight, palms up.

2.Raise the handles up to your shoulders, bending at your elbows. Do not sway back and forth as you lift and lower the handles.

3.Lower the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cable wood chop low to high
Cable Wrist Curl
description:
1.Sit with your forearms on your thighs holding straight bar out in front with your palms up and your wrists extended.

2. Flex at the wrists, raising the handle up as high as possible.

name: Calf Raise Farmer Walk
description:
1.Stand upright holding dumbbells on each hand, arms straight by your sides. Come up onto your toes, raising your heels off the floor.

2.Walk forward on your toes.

3.Move for a given distance, typically 50-100 feet, as fast as possible.

name: Cat to Cow
description:
1. When performing this mobility exercise, try to round out your lower back in one position and extend and open up your thoracic spine or the top part of your back in the other position.

2. Position yourself on the floor on your hands and knees.

3. Pull your belly in and round your spine, lowe

name: Child's Pose
description:
1. Get onto your hands and knees on the floor, with your hands slightly in front of your shoulders.

2. Sit your hips back towards your feet, while keeping your hands planted on the mat.

3. Hold this position while breathing and relaxing.

name: Chin Tuck Head Lift
description: Chin Tuck Head Lift

name: Chin Up
description:
1.Grab the pull-up bar with the palms facing your torso and a grip closer than the shoulder width, arms straight.

2.Pull your torso up until your head is around the level of the pull-up bar keeping elbows close to the body.

3.Lower your torso back to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Clamshell
description:
1. Lay on your side, and bend your knees keeping your legs stacked together.

2. Lift your top knee away from the bottom knee while holding the rest of your body in place, then return your knees together. 

3. Once you're done, repeat on the other side.

name: Clamshell with Hip Thrust
description:
1. Start on your side, with your elbow on the mat, knees stacked together, and feet behind you. 

2. Drive your hips up off the floor and forwards.

3. Open your knees while keeping your feet together.

4. Return to the start position, and repeat for desired number of repetitions. 

5. Repea

name: Clamshell with Twist
description:
1. Start on your side, with your elbow on the mat, knees stacked together, and feet behind you. 

2. Drive your top knee upward and away while keeping your feet together.

3. Holding the top knee in place, raise your top ankle towards the ceiling. 

4. Return to the start position and repeat f

name: Clapping Push Up
description:
1.Support your body on your toes and hands with your elbows bent and your chest nearly touching the floor.

2.Explosively push up and off the floor, clapping your hands as you come up.

3.Drop your hands back down and lower your body back to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Close Grip Scapular Retraction
description:
1.Sit holding the bar with your arms straight at shoulder height out in front and your back flat.

2.Squeeze your shoulder blades together as much as you can, keeping your arms straight. 

3.Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth.

name: Close Grip Strict Lat Pulldown
description:
1) Grasp the bar using a closed grip, palm facing away from you. Arms extended straight overhead.

2) Pull the bar down in front to the top of your chest.

3) Straighten your arms completely, returning the handle to the starting position.

4) Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and

name: Close Grip Weighted Pull Up
description:
1.Wearing a weight belt and using closed grip, grab a pull up bar. Palms facing away from you, arms and legs straightened making your body hang.

2.Pull your body upwards until your chin is just over the bar.

3.Lower your body returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Cobra
description:
1. Lie face down on your chest on a mat with your hands palms down next to your armpits. 

2. Press your hands into the floor and raise your upper body up away from the mat while keeping your hips on the mat. 

3. Relax and breathe in this position for desired amount of time.

name: Corpse Pose
description:
1. Lie on your back with your eyes closed, and hands out to your sides. 

2. Starting from head to toe, relax each individual part of your body one by one. 

3. Continue to breathe deeply and relax your whole body.

name: Cossack Squat to T-Spine Reach
description:
Start standing with feet wider than shoulder-width apart. Shift your weight onto one leg and perform a Cossack squat, bending that knee while keeping the other leg straight and toes pointing upward. 

While in the squat, reach the arm on the same side as the bent leg upward toward the ceiling, and

name: Crab alt knee tucks
description:
First set knee comes up 10/10 
Second set is 10 reps per leg one knee comes out, then straight,  out then straight.

name: Crab hold with alt knee raises
description: This looks easier then it is. Keep glutes tight and abs pulled in.

name: Crab Reach
description: In a crab position, lift your hips and reach one arm overhead, creating a full-body stretch.

name: Crab Walk
description:
(1) While seated on the ground, place your hands on the ground in line with your shoulders, feet under your knees. From here, lift your hips into the air, primarily from your glutes. 

(2) Take small alternating steps with your hands and feet, keeping your hips up. 

(3) Repeat until prescribed

name: Crocodile Breathing
description:
1. Lie on your chest, with your hands stacked, supporting your forehead.

2. Take a deep breath in, lasting about 4 seconds. Hold for a count of 2-4 seconds, then exhale for 4 seconds. 

3. Focus on expanding your ribcage in 360 degrees, as if you had a balloon inflating in your abdomen.

4. F

name: Cross Body Down Dog
description:
From downward dog, rotate your body and bring one leg across your body towards the opposite hand. 

This engages shoulders, core, and stretches the hips.

name: Cross Body Mountain Climber
description:
1) For this exercise, ensure that your abdominals are on and tight. 

2) Bring your knee from one side of your body towards the opposite elbow.

3) Move your feet as quickly as you can.

name: Cross Crunch
description:
1) For this exercise, you‚Äôll bring the opposite elbow to the opposing knee. 

2) Lie down on a mat. 

3) Slowly roll up and breathe out.

name: Cross Leg Stretch
description:
1.Lie on your back, cross your leg, with the ankle touching the knee. Arms straight out to the sides at shoulder height.

2.Drop the foot then grab the knee with one hand.

3.Pull the knee towards the floor, until you feel the stretch on the thighs.

4.Hold for a duration of time. Return to st

name: Crouching Tiger
description:
1) Start in a position similar to a plank, but with one side of your body's arm and leg far apart from one another (both extended), and one side of your body's arm and leg close together (touching knee to elbow on that side if possible). 

2) From here, lower your body to the ground staying as squ

name: Crunch Heel Tap
description:
1) For this exercise, shorten the distance between your pelvis and your rib cage. 

2) Try not to strain your neck by leaning forward with your head excessively. 

3) Lie down on a mat. 

4) Sit up and touch the heel alternately, left to right as you stay up and crunch forward.

name: Curl Up Crunch
description:
1.Lie on your back with your knees bent, feet flat, hands on your legs.

2.Curl neck towards your chest, and then slowly get your body off the floor until you reach the top position.

3.Lower body returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Curtsy Pulse Lunge
description:
Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart. Step one leg diagonally behind you and across the front leg into a curtsy lunge, bending both knees slightly.

From the bottom of the lunge, perform small pulses by moving up and down slightly without fully standing up.

Keep your chest upright, core en

Ho

name: Deadbug - Arms Only

Extend both arms straight toward the ceiling. Slowly lower one arm overhead toward the floor while keeping your lower back pressed into the ground and your legs stable. 

Return the

name: Deadbug - Legs Only
description:
Lie on your back with your arms extended toward the ceiling and knees bent at 90 degrees, shins parallel to the floor. 

Slowly extend one leg toward the floor, keeping the lower back pressed into the ground and the opposite leg stable. 

Return the leg to the starting position and repeat with t

name: Deadbug - Single Side
description:
Lie on your back with your arms extended toward the ceiling and knees bent at 90 degrees, shins parallel to the floor. 

Simultaneously extend one arm overhead and the opposite leg toward the floor, keeping your lower back pressed into the ground. 

Return to the starting position and repeat on

name: Deadbug Chest Press
description:
Lie on your back with your knees bent at 90 degrees and feet lifted off the floor. Hold a dumbbell in each hand at chest level, arms fully extended upward, palms facing forward.

name: Decline 2/3 leg press
description: Decline leg press. This is a 2/3 press which means you don‚Äôt fully complete the range of motion. Drop right before you would reach full extension with the legs. (Don‚Äôt straighten them fully)

name: Decline Bench Oblique Crunch
description:
1.Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and hands together behind your head.

2.Raise your upper body off the bench and slowly twist it to one side.

3.Lower your body back down slowly to the starting position as you inhale.

4.Repeat and alternate.

name: Decline Bench Sit Up
description:
1.Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and cross hands on your chest.

2.Lift your upper body completely off the bench, bringing your chest to your knees. Remember not pull your head up with your hands or throw yourself up off the bench.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Decline Mountain Climber
description:
Place your hands on the floor and your feet on a decline bench or elevated platform, keeping your body in a straight plank position. 

Drive one knee toward your chest, then quickly switch legs, alternating knees as fast as possible while maintaining a stable core throughout the movement.

name: Decline Plank to Pike
description:
Place your feet on an elevated platform or bench and your hands on the floor in a plank position, keeping your body in a straight line. 

Engage your core and lift your hips toward the ceiling, forming a pike position. Lower your hips back down into the plank and repeat continuously.

name: Decline Plank up to Pike
description:
Start in a plank position with your hands on the floor and feet on an elevated platform or bench. 

Lower your body onto your elbows, keeping your core engaged, then press back up to a hands plank. From there, lift your hips toward the ceiling into a pike position. 

Return to the hands plank an

name: Decline Push Up
description:
1.Using an object to create a decline with your body, support your body on your toes and hands with your feet on a step behind, elbows bent and your chest nearly touching the floor.

2.Push up to a straight arm position.

3.Lower your body back returning to starting position, keeping your back f

name: Decline Renegade Row
description:
Place two dumbbells on the floor and get into a plank position with your feet on an elevated platform or bench, keeping your body in a straight line and core tight. 

Row one dumbbell toward your hip while maintaining stability, then lower it back down and repeat on the other side, alternating row

name: Deficit Reverse Lunge
description:
Start standing on an elevated platform or step with both feet together. 

Step one foot backward into a reverse lunge, lowering your body while keeping your torso upright. 

Push through the front foot to return to the starting position on the platform.

name: Diagonal Bounds
description: Bound diagonally from one foot to the other, emphasizing explosive power.

name: Diamond Push Up
description:
1.Support your body on your toes and hands. Feet together and hands in diamond shaped placed under your chest.

2.Push up to a straight arm position, keeping your back flat and the diamond position of your hands.

3.Lower your body returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Dip
description:
1) Grip the dip bars with your hands shoulder width apart and arms fully extended. 

2) Begin by lowering your body until your upper arms are just below parallel to the ground, pause and return to starting position by pushing up with your triceps. 

3) Repeat.

name: Dip Machine Bent Leg Raise
description: 1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll want to bend and lift your knees up towards your shoulders by shortening the distance between your hips and your ribs, ideally without swinging.

name: Dip Machine Straight Leg Raise
description: 1)  When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll want to raise your legs up, ideally without swinging, shortening the distance between your hips and you ribs.

name: Dirty Dog
description:
1) To perform this exercise, get into a 4-point position. 

2) Make sure your hands are underneath your shoulders and abdominals are engaged. 

3) From here, isolate the movement from your hip and bring your knee out to the side of your body at roughly 45 degrees. 

4) Return.

description:
From a forearm plank, lift hips into a dolphin pose, then return to plank. 

Engage shoulders and core throughout.

name: Donkey Kick
description:
1) To perform this exercise, get into a 4-point position. 

2) Set your abdominals to make sure that your back stays in a neutral spine. 

3) From here, lift up your leg, keeping your knee bent by squeezing your bum.

name: Double Deadbug
description: Similar to the deadbug, but with both arms and legs extended simultaneously, maintaining control and stability through the core.

description:
1.Stand upright holding a kettlebell in each hand with your arms straight, in between your feet.

2.Bend at the hips and knees slightly then thrust your hips forward pulling one kettlebell straight up, catching it at shoulder height, absorbing it by dipping at the hips and knees slightly.

3.Low

name: Double Kettlebell Arnold Press
description:
1.Stand upright holding a kettlebell in each hand in the top position, resting at your biceps.

2.Looking straight ahead, press the kettlebell out and overhead.

3.Lower kettlebells down returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Double Kettlebell Clean
description:
1.Squat holding a kettlebell in each hand between your feet, arms straight.

2.Thrust your hips forward pulling the kettlebells straight up, catching them at shoulder height, bending at the elbows. Absorb the kettlebells at your shoulders by dipping at the hips and knees slightly.

3.Return to s

name: Double Kettlebell Clean to Front Squat
description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, kettlebells on the floor in front of you. Hinge at your hips and slightly bend your knees to grab the kettlebells. 

Explosively pull them up in a clean motion, bringing the kettlebells to shoulder height while keeping your elbows close to your body.

name: Double Kettlebell Push Press
description:
1.Stand upright, feet about shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand in the top position, resting at your biceps.

2.Squat down a few inches.

3.Use the momentum from the legs to drive the kettlebells overhead, legs straight.

4.Once the kettlebells are locked out, lower the ket

name: Double Kettlebell Racked Front Squat
description:
1.Stand upright, feet about shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand in the top position, resting at your biceps.

2.Looking straight ahead at all times, squat as low as you can and pause at the bottom.

3.Rise back up returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Double Kettlebell Squat
description:
1.Stand upright holding kettlebells on each hand in between your legs, feet flat, shoulder-width apart.

2.Lower your body toward the floor, sending your hips back and down and bending your knees. 

3.Push through your heels to return to the start position, keeping your back flat and head up thr

name: Double Kettlebell Swing
description:
1) Place two kettlebells between your feet. Push back with your butt, making sure that your back is flat and bend your knees to get into the starting position.

2) Swing the kettlebells between your legs forcefully. 

3) Quickly reverse the direction and drive through with your hips taking the k

name: Double Under
description:
1) The rope travels twice underneath you. 

2) Start the exercise with a single under to build momentum.

name: Dowel Around the World
description:
1) Place hands on dowel so it is waist height. 

2) With your arms remaining mostly straight, lift one arm overhead while leaving the other by your hip. 

3) As the top arm reaches overhead, and then behind your head, start the arm remaining by your hip in the same motion. 

4) The first movin

name: Dowel Behind the Head Slides
description:
1) Place hands wider than shoulder width apart on the dowel. 

2) Put the dowel overhead with straight arms.

name: Dowel Overhead Squat
description:
1) Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart.

2) Place your hands wider than shoulder width apart on the dowel. Move the dowel to the overhead position, biceps in line with ears.

3) With your back in neutral spine, sit your hips as far as possible while maintaining your correct post

name: Down Dog Single Leg Hip Opener to Climber
description:
Begin in downward dog position (hips lifted, arms and legs straight). 

Lift one leg toward the ceiling, then open your hips by rotating your leg outward. Hold for a stretch before switching legs. 

This targets hamstrings, hips, and shoulders.

name: Downward Dog
description:

3) Make a nice long line throughout your torso.

4) Stretch down the back of your thighs and your hamstrings. 

5) If that‚Äôs a little

name: Downward Dog Crossbody Reach
description: In downward dog, reach one hand across your body toward the opposite ankle for a deeper stretch.

name: Downward Dog to Push-Up
description: Transition from downward dog into a push-up position, then return.

name: Downward Dog to Scorpion
description: From downward dog, lift one leg and bend the knee, opening the hip for a scorpion stretch.

name: Duck stance DB row into RDL
Duck Walk
description:
1) Tuck arms under armpits, or place your hands on top of your head. Squat down as low as you can, getting your hips below your knees. 

2) Without standing out of your squat, step by shifting your weight and rotating at your hips, allowing you to walk. 

3) Repeat for the given distance, or rep

name: Dumbbell 1.5 Rep Goblet Squat
description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell vertically at your chest with both hands, elbows pointing down. Step your feet shoulder-width apart.

name: Dumbbell 21
description:
1. Stand upright and grab dumbbells with both hands. Place your hands shoulders-width apart and allow your arms to hang toward the floor.

2. Tuck your elbows tight to the sides of your body.

3. Curl upward until you make a 90-degree angle at your elbows. Relax your arms back to full extension

name: Dumbbell 6-Way Shoulder Raise
description:
Hold a dumbbell in each hand. Raise your arms straight in front to shoulder height, move them out to the sides, then overhead. 

Reverse the motion back to start,
maintaining control at all times.

name: Dumbbell 90 Degree Lateral Raise
description:
1.Stand upright holding the dumbbells out in front with your elbows bent at 90 degrees.

2.Raise your arms up and outward to the sides to shoulder height, keeping your elbows bent throughout.

3.Repeat.

name: Dumbbell Alternating Bicep Curl
description:
1) Stand up with arms hanging and dumbbells in both hands, palms facing in. 

2) Curl dumbbell for one arm, twisting your wrist on the way up and keeping your elbows still. 

3) Slowly lower and alternate with the other arm

name: Dumbbell Alternating Chest Press
description:
Lie on a bench with dumbbells in each hand, arms extended. 

Lower one dumbbell towards your chest while keeping the other arm extended, then press it back up and switch sides. 

This variation increases core engagement and stabilizer activation.

name: Dumbbell Alternating Curtsy Lunge
description:
Hold dumbbells at your sides and stand with feet hip-width apart. 

Step one leg behind and across the other into a curtsy lunge, then return to standing and repeat on the other side. 

Alternate legs with each rep, maintaining balance.

name: Dumbbell Alternating Floor Press
description:
1.Lie on the floor holding dumbbells at shoulder level with your elbows bent and your palms facing forward.

2.Press one dumbbell up over your chest, arm straight, while holding the other stationary.

3.Lower the pressed dumbbell back to shoulder level and immediately press the dumbbell with you

name: Dumbbell Alternating Hammer Curl
description:
1) Stand upright, holding a dumbbell in each hand, arms hanging at your sides and your palms inwards.

2) Curl the weight in one hand towards the shoulder in a circular motion.

3) Squeeze the bicep at the end of the lift and slowly lower. 

4) Alternate hands and repeat. Keep you body still,

name: Dumbbell Alternating Hammer Preacher Curl
description:
1.Kneel on the floor with your chest supported by a preacher bench, holding a dumbbell in each hand and arms resting on the pad.

2.Raise one dumbbell up to shoulder level, while leaving the other to starting position.

3.Alternate. Both dumbbells should be moving at the same time but in opposit

name: Dumbbell Alternating Lateral Lunge to Press
description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with palms facing inward. 

Step one foot out to the side into a lateral lunge, bending the lead knee while keeping the other leg straight. 

Push through the lead heel to return to the standing position, then press the dumbbells overh

name: Dumbbell Alternating Lateral Raise to Front Raise
description: Holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, lift the weights out to the sides up to shoulder height, then lower them down. Next, lift the weights in front of you to shoulder height and lower them again. Repeat the side and front raises alternately.

name: Dumbbell Alternating Preacher Bicep Curl
description:
1.Kneel on the floor with your chest supported by a preacher bench, holding a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up and arms resting on the pad.

2.Raise one dumbbell up to shoulder level, while leaving the other to starting position.

3.Alternate. Both dumbbells should be moving at the same ti

description: Using dumbbells, alternate stepping back into reverse lunges, curl the weights to your shoulders, then press the weights overhead.

description: Step back into a reverse lunge with one leg while curling the dumbbells to your shoulders at the same time. Stand up and repeat on the other leg, alternating sides.

2) Hold a dumbbell in each hand. 

3) Raise the dumbbells to your shoulders with your palms facing forward and your elbows pointed out. This will be your starting position. 

4) E

name: Dumbbell Alternating Tricep Press
description:
Perform a narrow chest press on a flat bench, but alternate arms with each rep. 

This increases the challenge on your core while focusing on the triceps and inner chest.

name: Dumbbell Atlas Press
description:
1.Stand upright holding one dumbbell in both hands at one shoulder with your elbows bent.

2.Press the dumbbell overhead, extending your arms fully

3.Lower the dumbbell down to the other side to shoulder height.

4.Repeat pressing the dumbbell up and over back to the start position. Alternate

name: Dumbbell Bench Deadbug Pullover
description:
Lie on a bench holding a single dumbbell with both hands, arms fully extended above your chest, palms facing each other. Lift your legs so your knees are bent at 90 degrees and your shins are parallel to the floor (deadbug position). 

Slowly lower one leg toward the floor while simultaneously low

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
description:
1) Lay on a flat bench with dumbbells in each hand. 

2) Bend your elbows at a 90-degree angle so that your forearms are perpendicular to the ground.

3) Press the weights upward until the two weights meet at the top. 

4) Pause and lower back to the start position.

2. Drive your hips forward, while keeping a rigid abdomen. 

3. Hold this position while breathing and staying tense.

4. Repeat on the other side.

description:
Hold a kettlebell in each hand at your sides. Step forward into a lunge with one leg, then alternate legs. 

This works quads, glutes, and enhances coordination.

2.Press one while holding the other kettlebell stationary.

3.Lower the pressed kettlebell and immediately press the kettlebell with your other arm.

4.Repeat.

name: Kettlebell Alternating Reverse Lunge
description:
Hold a kettlebell in each hand at your sides. Step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then alternate legs. 

This targets legs, glutes, and improves balance.

name: Kettlebell Alternating Rotational Press
description:
Start with a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height. 

Press one kettlebell overhead while rotating your torso towards the opposite side. 

Lower and repeat on the other side. This works the shoulders, core, and promotes rotational strength.

name: Kettlebell Alternating Stationary Cossack Squat
description:

description:

Hinge at your hips (not your back) and lower your torso, keeping your chest lifted and knees slightly bent. 

This strengthens your hamstrings, glutes, and lower back.

name: Kettlebell Arm Bar

Lie on your back and hold a kettlebell in one hand with your arm fully extended. 

Slowly rotate your body while keeping the kettlebell overhead. 

This works shoulders, stability, and mobility.

name: Kettlebell Bent Over Row
description:

This targets shoulders, arms, and core stability.

Engage your core and maintain balance while keeping the kettlebell steady.

Keep your chest lifted as you lower into the lunge, then return to standing.

Alternate sides.

Hinge at the hips, lowering the kettlebell, and return to standing.

Keep your chest upright and your core tight as you lower into the lunge. 

Return to standing and repeat on the other side.

name: Kettlebell Double Racked Curtsy Lunge
description:
Hold a kettlebell in the front rack position (near your shoulder) and step one leg behind in a curtsy lunge. 

Alternate legs while maintaining a strong posture.

2) Bend over by pushing your butt out and keeping your back flat. 

3) Pick up a kettlebell and pass it to your other hand between your legs. 

4) Go back and forth for several repetitions.

2.Press the kettlebells straight up over your chest, arms straight.

3.Lower the kettlebells back to shoulder level, returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

Press the kettlebell overhead. 

This strengthens the shoulders and engages the core.

Kneel with one knee on the ground and the other foot in front. 

Hold a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height, then press overhead. Lower and repeat. 

This targets shoulders and core while improving stability.

name: Kettlebell Ipsilateral Step Up

Press through the heel of the stepping leg to lift the body up, then step down and alternate legs.

name: Kettlebell Kickstand Deadlift
description:
Hold a kettlebell in each hand with your feet hip-width apart. 

Shift your weight to one leg, hinge at the hips, and lower the kettlebells toward the ground. 

This targets the hamstrings, glutes, and lower back.

name: Kettlebell Kickstand Deadlift

Stand with one leg in a kickstand position while holding a kettlebell in the opposite hand. 

Lower the kettlebell towards the ground while keeping your back straight, then return to standing.

Hold a kettlebell in each hand. Stand with one foot forward and the other foot behind you. 

Hinge forward at the hips while maintaining a neutral spine. 

This targets the hamstrings, glutes, and lower back.

Stand with one foot forward and the other foot lightly touching the floor behind you. Hold a kettlebell in each hand, and squat down on the front leg, keeping your chest upright. 

This focuses on the quads, glutes, and improves balance.

Hold a kettlebell in both hands. Stand on one leg, with the other leg slightly behind for balance. 

Swing the kettlebell between your legs, then thrust your hips forward to bring it to eye level. Focuses on the hips, glutes, and shoulders.

Kneel with one knee on the ground and the other foot in front. Hold a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height, then press overhead. Lower and repeat. 

This targets shoulders and core while improving stability.

Step out to the side with one leg, bending that knee while keeping the other leg straight. 

Push off the bent leg to return to standing. This targets the quads, glutes, and inner thighs.

Step sideways onto the bench with one foot, then bring the other foot to meet it. 

Step back down with the same foot. This targets the quads, glutes, and improves balance.

Step sideways onto the platform, then step down. Engages legs, glutes, and core.

description:

Alternate sides. 

This strengthens the core, shoulders, and improves stability.

description:
Swing the kettlebell up from the ground and ‚Äúpop‚ÄĚ it into the clean position by pulling your elbow high and keeping the kettlebell close to your body.

This works the legs, hips, and core while improving explosive power.

name: Kettlebell Pop Clean To Squat
description:
Swing the kettlebell up into the clean position, then transition into a squat. 

This strengthens legs, hips, and shoulders while improving movement flow.

name: Kettlebell Pullover Deadbug
description:
Perform a deadbug movement while holding a kettlebell in both hands. 

Extend one arm overhead and the opposite leg while keeping the kettlebell close to your body. 

This targets the core and shoulders.

name: Kettlebell Racked Alternating Forward Lunge
description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position. Step forward into a lunge, then alternate legs while maintaining a stable torso. 

This engages legs, glutes, and shoulders.

name: Kettlebell Racked Alternating Reverse Lunge
description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position. Step back into a reverse lunge with one leg, then alternate legs while maintaining good posture. 

This targets the legs, glutes, and shoulders.

name: Kettlebell Racked And Overhead Carry
description:
Hold one kettlebell in the rack position and the other overhead. 

Walk for a set distance, alternating the kettlebell positions. 

This targets shoulders, arms, and core stability.

name: Kettlebell Racked Bulgarian Split Squat
description:
Place one foot on an elevated surface. Hold a kettlebell in a racked position on the same side as the elevated foot. 

Lower into a split squat, keeping the chest up and engaging the core, then press back to standing.

name: Kettlebell Racked Carry
description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position (at shoulder height) and walk with it by your side. 

This engages the shoulders, arms, and core while improving stability.

name: Kettlebell Racked Forward Lunge
description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position at shoulder height. Step forward into a lunge, lowering your back knee toward the ground. Push back to standing.

This targets the legs and shoulders.

name: Kettlebell Racked Ipsilateral Forward Lunge
description:
Hold a kettlebell or dumbbell in a racked position on the same side as the lunging leg. 

Step forward into a lunge, lowering the back knee, then press back to standing.

name: Kettlebell Racked Kickstand Squat
description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position. Stand with one foot forward and the other foot slightly behind you. 

Squat down while keeping your chest lifted. This targets the legs and glutes, while engaging the shoulders.

name: Kettlebell Racked Lateral Step Up
description:
Stand on a platform with one foot. Lower your body by bending the knee of the standing leg, and step the other foot down to the ground. 

Step back up onto the platform with the same foot. This strengthens legs and improves lateral movement.

name: Kettlebell Racked Reverse Lunge
description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position at shoulder height. 

Step one leg back into a reverse lunge while keeping your chest upright. Return to the starting position. 

This focuses on quads, glutes, and core.

name: Kettlebell Racked Walking Lunges
description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position at shoulder height. 

Perform walking lunges while maintaining a stable torso. This targets the legs, glutes, and shoulders.

name: Kettlebell Renegade Row
description:
1) Support your body on your toes with your hands on kettlebells and your arms straight.

2) Raise one kettlebell up to your shoulder, bending your elbow, shifting your weight to the opposite side.

3) Lower the kettlebell and repeat on the other side.

4) Alternate sides with each rep.

name: Kettlebell Reverse Lunge
description:
Hold a kettlebell in each hand at your sides. 

Step one foot back into a lunge, bending both knees. Push back to the starting position. 

This targets quads, hamstrings, and glutes.

description: Perform a reverse lunge with one leg while pressing a kettlebell overhead with the opposite arm in a racked position.

name: Kettlebell Reverse Lunge with Ipsilateral Press
description: Perform a reverse lunge while pressing a kettlebell overhead with the same-side arm in a racked position.

description:
