name: Landmine Bulgarian Split Squat
description: Elevate your rear foot on a bench. Hold the bar at chest level. Lower into a Bulgarian split squat, keeping your torso upright.

name: Landmine Chest Fly Push-Up
description: Perform a push-up on the floor with hands gripping the barbell sleeves. As you push up, squeeze chest muscles inward for a fly effect.

name: Landmine Clean & Press
description: Clean the bar to shoulder height and press overhead. This is an explosive, full-body move.

name: Landmine Crunch
description: Landmine Crunch

name: Landmine Curtsy Lunges
description: Hold the bar with both hands and step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge. Keep the bar stable and chest upright.

name: Landmine Deadlift
description: Stand with feet hip-width apart. Hinge at the hips to lower the bar toward the ground, then stand back up by driving through the hips.

name: Landmine Drop Lunge to Press
description: Lunge backward and press the bar overhead in one motion. Return to standing and repeat.

name: Landmine Extended Rotations
description: Extend your arms further from your body while rotating the bar side-to-side to increase core challenge.

name: Landmine Front Squat
description: Hold the bar in a front-rack position. Keep your elbows high as you squat down. Maintain a tall chest and stable core throughout.

name: Landmine Glute Bridge
description: Lie on your back with feet flat and knees bent. Hold the bar across your hips and press upward into a glute bridge.

name: Landmine Good Morning
description: Hold the bar across your upper back. Hinge at the hips and lower your torso forward. Engage hamstrings and glutes to return upright.

name: Landmine Hack Squat
description: Stand holding the bar in front. Lean slightly back and squat, mimicking a hack squat motion. Drive through your heels to stand.

name: Landmine Half-Kneeling Single Arm Press
description: Kneel with one knee up (half-kneeling stance). Hold the barbell in the hand opposite the forward leg. Press upward and forward. Engage glutes and core to stabilize. Switch sides.

name: Landmine High Pull
description: Stand holding the bar with both hands. Explosively pull the bar up toward chest height, driving with hips and leading with elbows. Keep the bar close to the body.

name: Landmine Hip Thrust
description: Rest your upper back against a bench. Hold the bar at your hips and thrust your hips upward, squeezing the glutes at the top.

name: Landmine Iso Squat with Press
description: Hold the bar at shoulder height with both hands. Lower into a squat and press the bar as you hold the squat. Maintain an isometric squat while pressing.

name: Landmine Jump Squat
description: Hold the bar and perform a small jump from the squat position. Absorb the landing with soft knees.

name: Landmine kickstand Deadlift
description: Stagger your stance with one foot slightly back. Hinge at the hips and perform a deadlift, keeping the bar close. Alternate sides.

name: Landmine Kneeling Anti-Rotation
description: Kneel on both knees. Hold the bar in both hands and push it away while keeping your torso stable. Return with control.

name: Landmine Kneeling Hip Thrust
description: Kneel on both knees, holding the bar at chest height. Sit back onto your heels and drive up to full kneeling. Repeat.

name: Landmine Kneeling Shoulder Press
description: Kneel on both knees facing the bar, holding it at shoulder level with one or both hands. Press the bar upward and forward until the arm(s) are fully extended. Great for stability and shoulder work.

name: Landmine Lateral B Stance Deadlift
description: Perform a lateral lunge while holding the barbell, with a staggered stance for added stability. Focus on controlled movement and alternating sides.

name: Landmine Lateral Clean
description: Use an explosive hip drive to bring the bar laterally to shoulder height. Great for athletic power.

name: Landmine Lateral Curtsy Lunge to Knee Drive
description: Perform a side lunge and bring the rear leg into a curtsy position. Hold the bar for balance and resistance.

name: Landmine Lateral Deadlift
description:
Stand sideways to the barbell, holding it with one hand. 

Step out into a lateral lunge, lowering the barbell toward the floor. 

Push back to standing and alternate sides.

name: Landmine Lateral Hinge
description:
Hold the barbell with both hands and perform a lateral hip hinge, focusing on glutes and hamstrings. 

Return to standing and alternate sides.

name: Landmine Lateral Hop Press
description: Hold the bar in one hand. Perform a lateral hop and press the bar overhead. Land softly and repeat.

name: Landmine Lateral Lunge
description:
Hold the barbell with one hand at chest height. 

Step out to the side into a lunge, keeping the other leg straight. Push back to the starting position. Alternate sides.

name: Landmine Lateral Press
description: Hold the bar in one hand at shoulder height. Press the bar out laterally to the side, fully extending the arm. Engage your core to resist rotation.

name: Landmine Lateral Squat
description: Hold the barbell at chest height. Step out into a wide stance and perform a squat, focusing on shifting weight from one leg to the other.

name: Landmine Leg Raise
description: Lie on your back holding the bar vertically. Raise your legs toward the ceiling, keeping your core tight. Lower and repeat.

name: Landmine Low Lateral Lunge
description:
Hold the barbell with one hand at chest height. Step out to the side into a lunge, keeping the other leg straight. 

Push back to the starting position. Alternate sides.

name: Landmine Lunge to Press
description: Lunge forward and press the bar overhead as you rise from the lunge. Alternate sides.

name: Landmine Narrow Stance Hack Squats
description: Landmine Narrow Stance Hack Squats

name: Landmine Offset Deadlift to Press
description: Deadlift the bar and immediately press it overhead in one movement. Keep core tight throughout.

name: Landmine Offset Squat
description:
Hold the barbell with one hand at chest height. 

Perform a squat, focusing on maintaining balance and engaging your core. Alternate the holding hand.

name: Landmine Overhead Hold
description: Hold the bar overhead with both hands. Keep arms locked out and maintain the hold for time, focusing on shoulder and core stability.

name: Landmine Overhead Squat
description: Hold the bar overhead with both hands. Squat down as deeply as mobility allows while keeping the bar stable overhead.

name: Landmine Pistol Squat
description: Hold the bar in one hand. Stand on one leg and lower into a pistol squat. Use the bar for balance and assistance.

name: Landmine Pivot Press
description: Hold the bar and pivot your rear foot as you press the bar across your body. Involves full-body rotation.

name: Landmine Plank Position Row
description: Landmine Plank Position Row

name: Landmine Plank Row
description: In a plank position, row the bar with one hand while keeping hips stable. Alternate arms.

name: Landmine Power Clean
description: Drive the bar upward using full-body power, similar to a clean, but more focused on speed and explosiveness.

name: Landmine Press
description: Stand facing the landmine with the barbell end in both hands at chest level. Feet shoulder-width apart. Press the bar upward and forward until arms are fully extended. Lower with control. Great for shoulders and chest.

name: Landmine Push Press
description: Hold the bar at shoulder height. Dip slightly and drive the bar overhead using power from your legs.

name: Landmine RDL
description: Landmine RDL

name: Landmine RDL
description: Hold the bar with both hands. Hinge at the hips with a flat back, lowering the bar while keeping the bar close to your legs. Squeeze glutes to stand.

name: Landmine Rear Delt Fly
description: Bend at the hips with a flat back, holding the bar in one hand. Raise the arm out to the side in a reverse fly motion, targeting the rear delts. Lower with control.

name: Landmine Reverse Lunge
description: Hold the bar at chest level. Step out laterally into a side lunge, keeping the bar centered. Push back to the start position.

name: Landmine Rollout
description: Hold the bar end with both hands from a kneeling or standing position. Roll the bar forward, extending your body, then pull it back.

name: Landmine Romanian Deadlift
description:
Place one end of a barbell into a landmine attachment. 

Stand facing the barbell, grab it with both hands, and hinge at the hips to lower the barbell. 

Return to standing by engaging your glutes and hamstrings.

name: Landmine Rotation
description: Hold the barbell with both hands. Rotate your torso from side to side, keeping your core engaged and controlling the movement.

name: Landmine Rotation Lunge
description: Hold the bar at shoulder height. Step forward into a lunge and rotate your torso over the front leg. Return and repeat.

name: Landmine Rotation To Press
description:
Hold the barbell with both hands. Rotate your torso to one side, then return to center and press the barbell overhead. 

Repeat on the other side.

name: Landmine Rotational Clean and Press
description: Clean the bar to your shoulder using hip drive, then rotate and press across the body. Repeat fluidly.

name: Landmine Rotational Press
description: Start in an athletic stance holding the bar with both hands near one shoulder. Rotate your torso and press the bar across your body to the opposite side. Return to start. Repeat and switch sides.

name: Landmine Rotations
description: Hold the end of the bar with both hands and rotate it side-to-side in a controlled arc. Keep core engaged.

name: Landmine Russian Twist
description: Sit down and hold the bar with both hands. Rotate your torso side-to-side like a Russian twist, tapping the bar end to the floor.

name: Landmine Shoulder Press
description:
Hold the barbell with one hand at shoulder height. 

Press the barbell upward and slightly forward. 

Lower with control and alternate arms.

name: Landmine Shoulder to Shoulder Press
description: Hold the barbell with both hands close to your chest. Shift the bar from one shoulder to the other in a pressing arc, alternating sides. Keep elbows slightly bent and core engaged.

name: Landmine Side Bend
description: Hold the bar and bend laterally at the waist. Focus on stretching and strengthening the obliques.

name: Landmine Side Lunge
description: Hold the bar at chest level. Step out laterally into a side lunge, keeping the bar centered. Push back to the start position.

name: Landmine Side Plank Hold
description: Hold the bar with one hand while in a side plank. Maintain the position to strengthen the obliques and shoulders.

name: Landmine Side-to-Side Lateral Squat
description: Landmine Side-to-Side Lateral Squat

name: Landmine Single Arm Bicep Curl
description: Stand facing the bar and hold with an underhand grip. Curl the bar toward your shoulders, keeping elbows tucked. Squeeze the biceps at the top, then lower slowly.

name: Landmine Single Arm Bicep Row
description: With a bent-over stance, hold the bar with an underhand grip (palms up). Row the barbell toward your lower chest, squeezing shoulder blades together. Control the descent.

name: Landmine Single Arm Push Press
description:
Similar to the push press but performed with one arm. 

Use your legs to assist in driving the barbell overhead. 

Alternate arms.

name: Landmine Single Arm Row
description: Stand beside the bar. Hinge at the hips and hold the bar with the inside hand. Row the bar up toward your hip, keeping your elbow close. Pause, then lower. Switch sides.

name: Landmine Single Arm Row
description:
Hold the barbell with one hand while in a bent-over position. 

Pull the barbell toward your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body. 

Alternate arms.

name: Landmine Single Arm Shoulder Press
description:
1)  When performing this exercise, ensure a stable base and a stable position with your feet. 

2) Keep your core engaged as you press and lock out with your arm. 

3) Make sure that as you press, your arm is in line with your body.

name: Landmine Single Arm Tricep Extension
description: Stand holding the bar behind your head with both hands, elbows pointing up. Extend your arms to press the bar overhead. Focus on isolating the triceps during the press.

name: Landmine Single Arm Upright Row
description: Hold the bar with both hands close together. Pull the bar straight up to chest height, leading with your elbows. Keep it close to your body throughout the movement.

name: Landmine Single Leg Deadlift
description:
Hold the barbell in one hand and stand on the opposite leg. Hinge forward, keeping your back flat and the barbell close to your body. 

Return to standing and alternate legs.

name: Landmine Single Leg Deadlift (side facing)
description: Hold the bar in one hand. Balance on the opposite leg and hinge at the hips, lowering the bar while extending the free leg back. Return to stand.

name: Landmine Single Leg Hip Thrust
description: Set up like a hip thrust, but keep one foot grounded and the other leg extended. Drive hips up using one leg. Repeat and switch.

name: Landmine Single-Arm Press
description: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding the landmine barbell in one hand at shoulder level. Press the bar upward and slightly forward. Keep your core tight and avoid leaning back. Switch arms.

name: Landmine Skater Squat
description: Hold the bar and perform a skater squat, lowering your back knee without touching the floor. Keep the bar stable throughout.

name: Landmine Snatch
description: Explosively pull the bar up and overhead in one motion using hips and shoulders. A power-based move.

name: Landmine Split Squat
description: Place one foot forward and one back in a split stance. Hold the bar at chest level. Lower into a split squat and return to start.

name: Landmine Split Squat
description:
Hold the barbell at chest height and step one foot back into a split stance. 

Lower your back knee toward the ground, then push through the front foot to return. Alternate legs.

name: Landmine Split Squat to Single Arm Press
description:
1) For this exercise, you‚Äôll perform a full lunge maintaining the body weight over top of your back knee not lunging forward with your front knee. 

2) Extend up and locking up. 

3) When you lock out into the shoulder press, ensure that your arm is in line with your body and not out to the side

name: Landmine Split Stance Single Arm Row
description:
Perform a bent-over row while in a split stance. 

Hold the barbell with both hands and pull it toward your chest. 

Alternate leg stances.

name: Landmine Squat
description: Hold the bar at chest height with both hands. Squat down, keeping your chest up and heels grounded. Drive up to standing. Repeat.

name: Landmine Squat to High Pull
description: Hold the bar. Squat down and then pull the bar explosively up toward your chin. A combo of lower and upper body work.

name: Landmine Squat To Press
description:
Hold the barbell at chest height. 

Perform a squat, then push through your heels to stand while pressing the barbell overhead in one motion.

name: Landmine Squat to Single Arm Press
description:
1)  When performing the squat, sit your hips back behind you. Ensure that your knees don‚Äôt come over top of your toes.

2) Keep your chest up and your back set. 

3)  When you do your press, ensure that your arm is in line with your body rather than out to the side.

name: Landmine Standing Pallof Press
description: Stand and hold the bar at chest height with both hands. Press the bar forward and hold, resisting rotation. Return to start.

name: Landmine Step-Through Lunge
description: Hold the bar at your side and step forward and across into a lunge, passing the bar underneath the front leg as you go. Repeat.

name: Landmine Sumo Deadlift
description: Set your feet wider than shoulder-width. Hold the bar with both hands. Perform a deadlift by hinging at the hips, then stand tall.

name: Landmine Sumo Squat
description:
Stand with a wide stance and hold the barbell with both hands at chest height. 

Squat down deeply, keeping your back straight. 

Push through your heels to return.

name: Landmine T-Bar Close Grip Row
description:
1)  To perform this exercise, find the double grip row handle from the cable machine. 

2) Place it under the barbell. 

3) Sit your back with a slight arch. 

4) Squeeze between the shoulder blades to perform the rowing motion.

name: Landmine Thruster
description: Hold the bar at chest level. Squat down and press the bar overhead as you stand. A total-body power move.

name: Landmine V-Sit Shoulder Press
description: Sit in a V-sit position. Press the bar overhead while maintaining balance and core engagement. Alternate sides or reps.

name: Lat Machine Parallel Grip Row
description:
1.Lean back and look up while holding the handles using a closed grip, with arms extended straight overhead.

2.Pull the handles down in front to the top of your chest, lean back.

3.Straighten your arms completely, returning the handle to the starting position.

4.Do not sway back and forth.

name: Lat Machine Single Arm Close Grip Row
description:
1.Grasp the handle with your arm extended straight overhead, palm facing in. Lean back slightly and look up.

2.Pull the handle down in front to the top of your chest.

3.Straighten your arm, returning the handle to the starting position, going through a full range of motion. 

4.Repeat. Compl

name: Lat Machine Single Arm Wide
description:
1.Grasp the handle in one hand with your arm extended straight overhead, palm facing forward. Keep upright back position.

2.Pull the handle down, allowing the elbow wide and outside away from the body.

3.Return the handle back up to the start position, straightening your arm.

4.Repeat. Comp

name: Lat Machine Single Arm Wide Grip
description:
1.Grasp the handle with your arm extended straight overhead, palm facing in.

2.Pull the handle down in front to the top of your chest.

3.Straighten your arm, returning the handle to the starting position, going through a full range of motion.

4.Repeat. Complete all reps on one side before s

name: Lat Machine Single Arm Wide Row
description:
1.Lean back and look up while holding the handle in one hand with your arm extended straight overhead, palm facing forward.

2.Pull the handle down, allowing the elbow wide and outside away from the body.

3.Straighten your arm fully, returning the handle to the top position.

4.Repeat. Comple

name: Lat Machine Straight Arm Pulldown
description:
1.Grip the bar in both hands at chest level with your elbows bent.

2.Push the bar down, fully extending your arms and keeping your shoulders steady.

3.Your hands should finish at the top of your thighs in the downward position.

4. Return to starting position.

5. Repeat.

name: Lat Machine Wide Grip Scapular Retraction
description:
1.Sit upright holding the handle using a wide grip with your arms straight out in front and your back flat.

2.Pull your shoulder blades together as much as you can, keeping your arms straight. Remain upright throughout and do not sway back and forth.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Lat Pulldown Machine Wide Grip
description:
1) Grasp the bar using a shoulder-width grip with your arms extended straight overhead.

2) Pull the bar down in front to the top of your chest, bending at the elbows.

3) Straighten your arms fully, returning the bar to the top position.

name: Lat Stretches & Exercises (Latissimus dorsi) - Ask Doctor Jo
description: Lat Stretches & Exercises (Latissimus dorsi) - Ask Doctor Jo

name: Lateral Bear Crawl
description: Get into a bear crawl position and move laterally, keeping the knees low and core engaged.

name: Lateral Bound to Jump Switch
description: From a standing position, bound laterally to one side, landing softly, then immediately perform a small jump switch to alternate legs before bounding to the other side.

name: Lateral Bounds
description: Jump laterally from side to side, landing softly on each side while keeping your knees slightly bent.

name: Lateral Gorilla Hops
description: Squat low and hop laterally, using your arms for momentum.

name: lateral kicks
description: lateral kicks

name: Lateral Leg Swing
description:
1. When performing this exercise, try to keep the knees straight on the leg that you're swinging.

2. Hold on to something for balance.

3. Stand on one leg. This will be your starting position.

4. Keeping your leg straight, raise it as far out to the side as possible, and swing it back down,

name: Lateral Lunge to Hop
description:
Start standing tall with feet hip-width apart. Step one foot out laterally into a deep lunge, keeping the other leg straight and chest upright. 

Push through the lunging leg to return to standing, then immediately hop off the ground, landing softly back into the starting position

name: Lateral Lunge to Side Kick
description: Perform a lateral lunge, then transition the extended leg to become the standing leg and execute a side kick with the opposite leg, returning to the starting position.

name: Lateral Lunge to T-Rotation
description:
Step sideways into a lateral lunge, then rotate your torso toward the bent leg, reaching one arm upward. 

Return and repeat on the other side.

name: Lateral Shuttle Run
description:
Stand with feet hip-width apart. Push your hips back, keeping your back flat, and bend at the hips (not the knees) to lower your torso. 

Return to standing by pushing your hips forward. This targets the hamstrings, glutes, and core.

name: Lateral Sled Drag
description:
1) When performing this exercise, you‚Äôll want to step as far to the side of yourself as you can.

2) Try not to step forwards or backwards.

3) From there, engage your abdominals and lean away from the sled.

name: Lateral Stepover to Push-Up
description:
Start standing on a bench or elevated platform. Step one foot off the bench to the side, then bring the other foot down to follow so you are on the floor. 

Immediately drop into a push-up position and perform a push-up. Step back up onto the bench and repeat continuously, alternating sides.

name: Lateral Step-Up to High Knee
description:
Start standing with both feet on a bench. 

Step one foot off the bench to the side (lateral movement), then immediately drive the knee of that same foot toward your chest. 

Return both feet to the bench and repeat.

name: Lateral/ frontal slow eccentric raises
description: Later raise, rotate forward into frontal raise position , raise all the way up over head, slower slowly 4 count. Repeat.

name: Laying Hamstring Curl
description: Lie face down on the hamstring curl machine. Place your ankles under the pad. Curl the pad toward your glutes by bending your knees, then slowly lower back down.

name: Leg Press Machine Calf Raise
description:
1. Sit on a leg press machine, and place your feet against the pad about shoulder width apart. Make sure your knees are bent at the start of the exercise. 

2. Slowly push against the pad using the palm of your feet until your knee is almost straight. Then move back into the start position. Do not

name: Leg Press Machine Calf Raise
description: In the leg press machine with legs extended, press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels. Squeeze your calves, then lower slowly.

name: Leg Press Machine Single Leg
description:
1. Sit on a leg press machine, and place one foot against the pad about shoulder width apart. Make sure your knee is bent at the start of the exercise. 

2. Slowly push against the pad using your heel until your knee is  almost straight. Do not lock your knee straight at the peak of this exercise.

name: Leg Press Machine Single Leg Calf Raise
description:
1. Sit on a leg press machine and press against the pad with your foot until your leg is almost straight. Then slide your feet down so that your heel is hanging off. 

2. Begin by pushing against the pad with your toes as far as you can go, pause, and return to the starting position.  

3. Perfo

name: Leg Raise to Hip Lift
description:
Lie on your back with legs extended and arms by your sides. Engage your core and lift your legs toward the ceiling while keeping them straight (leg raise). 

Once your legs are vertical, lift your hips off the ground, driving them upward to form a slight posterior tilt, then slowly lower both hips

name: Leopard Crawl
description: Crawl forward while keeping your knees just above the ground, engaging your core and shoulders.

name: Low leg lift with glute raise
Low plank Jacks
description:
Begin in a low plank position on your forearms. 

Jump both feet out wide, then back to the starting position. Keep your core tight and back straight throughout the movement.

name: Low Plank Single Leg Lift Hold
description: Hold a low plank position and lift one leg off the ground, maintaining stability through the core and keeping the body in a straight line.

name: Low Pull Technogym
description: Low Pull Technogym

name: Lower Calf Stretch
description:
1. For this stretch, you'll want to find a ledge or a step or if you're at home, you can use the wall.

2. Keep your heel on the ground at all times while you stretch.

3. Let your knee bend, push forward towards your toes.

4. You'll feel the stretch a little bit lower down closer to your ank

name: Lower leg lifts with glute raise
Lunge Tap Ins
description: Step back into a lunge, then tap your back foot in next to your front foot. Return to the lunge. Repeat.

name: Lunge to Curtsy
description:
Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart. Step one leg back into a reverse lunge, bending both knees and lowering your body while keeping your torso upright and core engaged. 

From the bottom of the reverse lunge, transition directly into a curtsy lunge by moving the same leg diagonally behind t

name: Lying Barbell or EZ Bar Triceps Extension
description: Lying Barbell or EZ Bar Triceps Extension

name: Lying Floor Rope Pulls
description:
1. Start by lying on your back, holding the rope in both hands.

2. Pull yourself up hand-over-hand, until you reach a standing position.

3. Slowly lower yourself back to the starting position and repeat.

name: Lying Hip Abductions
description:
1. Start by lying on your side on the mat, with your elbow supporting your head.

2. Stack your knees, and bend the knee closest to floor, bringing that foot behind your body. 

3. Slowly raise the foot of your top leg into the air, directly in line with your body, then return to the start posit

name: Lying Machine Abdominal Crunch
description:
1. Lie on the abdominal crunch machine, following the directions for the correct start position. 

2. Squeeze your abdomen, bringing your ribs down towards your pelvis. 

3. Slowly return to the start position under control and repeat.

name: Lying Piriformis Stretch
description:
1. For this stretch, you'll feel it in the side of the hip on the leg that's crossover your other leg.

2. There are 3 positions that get your to a deeper and deeper stretch. 

3. Lie down with your one knee bent and the other foot crossover the bent knee.

4. Once you've stretched out the lef

name: Lying Single Leg Raise
description:
1. For this exercise, ensure your abdominals are active as your leg approaches the ground as well as when it's leaving the ground.

2. Lie with your back flat on a ground and your legs extended in front of you.

3. Place your hands on your sides with your palms down.

4. As you keep your legs

name: Lying Slider Runner
description:
1.When performing this exercise, ensure that you bridge up and keep your glutes active throughout the whole exercise.

2.Try not to let them sit down towards the mat as you alternate from left to right foot. 

3.You‚Äôll also wanna keep your abdominals engaged to help preventing from lifting from

name: Lying Straight Leg Raise
description:
1. For this exercise, keep your core engaged, especially when you're lifting your feet up off the floor but also while they're returning and getting close to the ground again.

2. Lie with your back flat on a ground and your legs extended in front of you.

3. Place your hands on your sides with

name: Lying T-Bar Row
description: Rest your upper chest against the top of the pad and grab the handles. Pull the weight up and squeeze your back at the top of the movement. Pause, and return to the starting position.

name: Machine Assisted Chin Up
description:
1. Start by kneeling on the pad of the assisted chin-up machine. Grab the handles with your palms facing towards you. 

2. Slowly lower yourself until your elbows are completely straight, and you're in a hanging position. 

3. Pull yourself up as high as you can under control. Repeat. 

4. Rem

name: Machine Assisted Close Grip Pull Up
description:
1) Select your appropriate weight for the exercise on the machine. Using more weight makes it easier. 

2) Grab the pull up bar with hands facing each other, on the narrow handles. Place your knees on the machine's rest pad and slowly load your body weight onto the machine. Let your arms go to ful

name: Machine Assisted Dip
description:
1) Select desired weight and grip the dip bars with hands shoulder width apart and knees on support pad. 

2) Begin by lowering your body until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.

3) Pause and return to starting position by pushing up with your triceps. Repeat.

name: Machine Assisted Parallel Grip Pull Up
description:
1) Select your appropriate weight for the exercise on the machine. Using more weight makes it easier. 

2) Grab the pull up bar with fists facing one another. Place your knees on the machine's rest pad and slowly load your body weight onto the machine. Let your arms go to full extension, and your

name: Machine Assisted Wide Grip Pull Up
description:
1) Select your appropriate weight for the exercise on the machine. Using more weight makes it easier. 

2) Grab the pull up bar with hands facing forward. Place your knees on the machine's rest pad and slowly load your body weight onto the machine. Let your arms go to full extension, and your body

name: Machine Back Extension
description: Adjust seat so back pad fits between shoulder blades. Legs should be slightly bent. Place feet firmly on foot pad and move torso back slowly. Pause at extended position.

name: Machine Bicep Curl
description:
1. Start seated on the bicep curl machine.

2. Grasp the handles and curl your palms upwards using your biceps.

3. Pause, and return to starting position. Repeat.

name: Machine Glute Kickback
description:
1. Start by resting your chest against the body pad, grasping the handles, with a single foot on the weight plate. 

2. Push the plate away, fully extending your knee and your hip. Squeeze the glutes for a moment, then return to the start position. 

3. Repeat on the other leg.

name: Machine Incline Chest Press
description:
1.When performing this exercise, you wanna set up the machine so that the handles are in-line with the middle of your chest at the bottom of the press.

2.Also, make sure that the weights don‚Äôt tap down in between every repetition.

3.Start by pressing handles out until arms are extended.

4.R

name: Machine Lateral Raise
description:
1. Start seated on the lateral raise machine. Place your hands on the handles, and keep your elbows in contact with the pads. 

2. Drive your elbows upwards and outwards until your upper arm is parallel to the floor. 

3. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat.

name: Machine Lying Leg Curl
description:
1.When performing the leg curl, what you wanna do is ensure that the bottom pad is just above the ankles and the top pad is above your knee. 

2.You don‚Äôt want the pad resting on your knee cap.

3.Also, ensure that the weights don‚Äôt tap down between your repetitions.

4. Keeping the torso flat

name: Machine Lying Single Leg Curl
description:
1.For this exercise, you‚Äôll be working the hamstrings.

2.Ensure that the top pad is above your knee cap to prevent injury.

3.Also, make sure the weights don‚Äôt tap down in between every single repetition.

name: Machine Preacher Curl
description: Sit with your upper arms resting on the preacher pad. Grip the bar and curl it toward your shoulders. Squeeze your biceps, then lower under control.

name: Machine Seated Abduction
description:
1.Sit upright in the machine with the leg pads at the outside of your knees with your knees together and your feet on the foot pegs.

2. Push your knees apart, bringing the pads away from each other.

3. Bring your knees back together, returning to starting position. Repeat.

name: Machine Seated Calf Raise
description:
1.When performing this exercise, put the ball of your foot on the step. 

2.Place your lower thighs under the lever pad, which will need to be adjusted according to the height of your thighs. Now place your hands on top of the lever pad in order to prevent it from slipping forward. 

3.In order

name: Machine Seated Chest Fly
description:
1.Sit at the machine with your back flat on the pad. Both hands holding the handles with elbows slightly bent.

2.Push the handles together and squeeze your chest once your forearms reach the middle. Pause.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Machine Seated Chest Press
description:
1) Sit upright making sure back is rested on the backrest, both hands holding the horizontal position handles and close to your shoulders, elbows bent.

2) Push both handles forward to a straight arm position in front of your chest.

3) Bending at the elbows, slowly return to starting position.

name: Machine Seated Dip
description:
1) Sit at the machine and grasp the hand grips which should be slightly above elbow level. 

2) Begin by pressing handles downwards until your arms are almost straight, pause, and return to starting position.

name: Machine Seated Dips
description: Sit on the dip machine with your hands on the handles and elbows bent. Push the handles down to straighten your arms, then slowly return to the start.

name: Machine Seated Hip Adduction
description:
1. Sit upright in the machine with the leg pads at the inside of your knees with your knees apart and your feet on the foot pegs.

2. Drive your knees inwards, bringing the pads close together in the middle.

3. Bring your knees outwards, returning to starting position. Repeat.

name: Machine Seated Leg Curl
description:
1.Adjust the machine lever to fit your height and sit on the machine with your back against the back support pad. Legs fully straight in front of you, back of lower legs on top of the padded lever. Grasp the side handles on the machine as you point your toes straight.

2.Curl your legs downward as

name: Machine Seated Leg Extension
description:
1) For this exercise, ensure that the pad rests just above the ankle.

2) Secondly, you wanna make sure that the weights don‚Äôt tap down in between each repetition.

3) Keep a tall body throughout the exercise. 

4) Extend your knees fully, raising your legs straight out.

name: Machine Seated Neutral Grip Row
description:
1.Sit at a reverse position in a machine, chest against the backrest. Hands holding the vertical position handle, arms extended.

2.Pull the handles towards your chest, bending at the elbows. Pause.

3.Slowly return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Machine Seated Neutral Grip Row
description:
1.Sit at a reverse position in a machine, chest against the backrest. Hands holding the vertical position handle, arms extended.

2.Pull the handles towards your chest, bending at the elbows. Pause.

3.Slowly return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Machine Seated Parallel Grip Chest Press
description:
1.Sit upright making sure back is rested on the backrest, both hands holding the handles and close to your shoulders, elbows bent.

2.Push both handles forward to a straight arm position in front of your chest.

3.Bending at the elbows, slowly return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Machine Seated Parallel Grip Shoulder Press
description:
1.Sit upright at a shoulder press machine, holding the inside handles at parallel grip position, fists facing each other and your elbows bent.

2.Press the handles overhead, extending your arms fully.

3.Lower the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Machine Seated Reverse Fly
description:
1.Sit at a reverse position in a machine, chest against the back rest. Both hands holding the handles, elbows slightly bent.

2.Move your arms outward and backward in a reverse fly motion until your elbows go just past your back. Pause.

3.Slowly return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Machine Seated Shoulder Press
description:
1.Sit upright at a shoulder press machine, holding the inside handles at shoulder height with your palms facing in and your elbows bent.

2.Press the handles overhead, extending your arms fully.

3.Lower the handles returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Machine Seated Single Arm Bicep Row
description:

3.Slowly return to starting position.

4.Comple

name: Machine Seated Single Arm Chest Fly
description:

3.Return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before

name: Machine Seated Single Arm Neutral Grip Row
description:
1.Sit at a reverse position in a machine, chest against the backrest. One hand holding the vertical position handle, arm extended.

2.Pull the handle towards your chest, bending at the elbow. Pause.

3.Slowly return to starting position.

name: Machine Seated Single Arm Parallel Grip Chest Press
description:

4.Complete all reps on one

name: Machine Seated Single Arm Parallel Grip Shoulder Press
description:
1.Sit upright at a shoulder press machine, holding the inside handle at parallel grip position, elbow bent.

2.Press the handle overhead, extending your arm fully.

3.Lower the handle returning to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

name: Machine Seated Single Arm Reverse Fly
description: Sit facing the machine with one hand on the handle. With a slight bend in your elbow, sweep the arm out and back. Squeeze your shoulder blade at the top.

name: Machine Seated Single Arm Shoulder Press
description:
1.Sit upright at a shoulder press machine, with one hand holding the inside handle at shoulder height, palm facing in and elbow bent.

2.Press the handle overhead, extending your arm fully.

3.Lower the handle returning to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to

name: Machine Seated Single Arm Supinated Grip Row
description:
1.Sit at a reverse position in a machine, chest against the backrest. One hand holding the horizontal position handle, shoulder parallel to the hand.

2.Pull the handle into you and try to squeeze your shoulder blades together in the back Pause.

3.Slowly return to starting position.

4.Comple

3.In order to

name: Machine Seated Single Leg Curl

1.Adjust the machine lever to fit your height and sit on the machine with your back against the back support pad. Leg fully straight in front of you, back of the lower leg on top of the padded lever. Grasp the side handles on the machine as you point your toes straight.

name: Machine Seated Supinated Grip Row
description:

name: Machine Single Leg Extension
description:

name: Machine Standing Calf Raise
description: Stand straight at a standing calf raise machine with your shoulders under the pads and your heels hanging off the foot step. Begin by raising up on your toes as high as you can go, pause, and then return to the starting position.

name: Machine Standing Single Leg Curl
description:
1) Stand straight at a standing leg curl machine with the pad behind one of your ankles. 

2) Begin by curling your leg up as far as you can, pause, and return to starting position. 

3) After you finish the desired number of reps, switch legs.

name: Machine Sumo Leg Press
description:
1. Sit on a leg press machine, and place your feet against the pad wider than shoulder-width apart. Make sure your knees are bent at the start of the exercise.

2. Slowly push against the pad using your heels until your knees are almost straight. Do not lock your knees straight at the peak of this

name: Machine Tricep Pushdowns
description:
1. Start seated on the tricep pushdown machine, with your hands on the handles. 

2. Slowly raise the weight so that your forearms come up to touch your biceps, while your elbows move backwards. 

3. Push the handles all the way down until your elbows are straight. Repeat.

name: Machine Weighted Back Extensions
description: Lie face down on the hamstring curl machine. Place your ankles under the pad. Curl the pad toward your glutes by bending your knees, then slowly lower back down.

name: Marching Glute Bridge
description: Perform a glute bridge while alternating marching your legs in the air one at a time.

name: Marching On The Spot
description: March in place, focusing on controlled movements and alternating legs while engaging the core.

name: Medicinal Ball Alternating Lunge
description:
Hold a medicine ball in front of you. 

Step forward into a lunge with one leg, then return to standing and alternate with the other leg, while keeping the ball in front of you.

name: Medicinal Ball Alternating Reverse Lunge
description:
Hold a medicine ball at chest height. 

Step backward into a reverse lunge, alternating legs with each repetition, ensuring a deep lunge and proper form.

name: Medicine Ball Alternating Lunge And Twist
description: Perform a lunge while holding a medicine ball. As you lunge, twist your torso over the front leg, alternating sides with each lunge and twist.

name: Medicine Ball Alternating Overhead Lunge
description:
Hold a medicine ball overhead. 

Step into a forward lunge, alternating legs, while keeping the ball overhead for stability and core engagement.

name: Medicine Ball Alternating Overhead Reverse Lunge
description: Hold a medicine ball overhead. Step back into a reverse lunge, alternating legs, keeping the ball overhead and engaging the core throughout.

name: Medicine Ball Alternating Split Jumps
description:
Hold a medicine ball close to your chest. Start in a split stance with one foot forward and one back. 

Jump explosively, switching your legs in mid-air, and land softly in a split stance on the opposite side. 

Keep the ball steady as you perform the jumps.

name: Medicine Ball Calf Raises
description: Hold a medicine ball at your chest. Perform calf raises by lifting your heels off the ground and lowering slowly.

name: Medicine Ball Flutter Kicks
description: Lie on your back with a medicine ball on your chest. Perform flutter kicks, keeping your core engaged.

name: Medicine Ball Half Kneel Wood Chop
description:
Kneel on one knee with a medicine ball in both hands. 

Twist your torso as you bring the ball from a high position to a low one, focusing on the obliques.

name: Medicine Ball Half Kneeling Alternating Slams
description:
Kneel with one knee on the ground and the other foot planted in front for stability. Hold a medicine ball with both hands. 

Alternate slamming the ball to the ground on either side of your body, using a full range of motion from overhead to the floor. 

Focus on controlled, explosive power with

name: Medicine Ball Hollow Hold Press
description:
Lie on your back holding a medicine ball close to your chest. Lift your shoulders and legs off the floor, keeping your lower back pressed into the ground to form a hollow position. 

While maintaining core tension, press the medicine ball straight up over your chest, then lower it back down with c

name: Medicine Ball Knee Drives
description: Hold a medicine ball overhead. Drive one knee up to your chest while pulling the ball down. Alternate legs.

name: Medicine Ball Kneeling Slam
description:
In a kneeling position, hold a medicine ball above your head. 

Slam it to the ground with force, using your core and upper body, then catch and repeat.

name: Medicine Ball Leg Drops
description: Hold a medicine ball with your feet. Lower your legs toward the floor, then raise them back up while engaging your core.

name: Medicine Ball Low Plank Roll Outs
description: From a low plank, place forearms on a medicine ball. Roll the ball forward, then pull it back while engaging your core.

name: Medicine Ball Lunge
description:
1.Stand upright holding the ball with both hands, just under the chin and against your chest.

2.Take a step forward, dropping your back knee toward the floor, keeping the ball against your chest.

3.Push off your front foot returning to starting position.

4.Repeat and alternate.

name: Medicine Ball Lunge Twist to Overhead Press
description:
Stand tall holding a medicine ball at chest level. Step one foot back into a reverse lunge and, as you lower down, rotate your torso toward your front leg while keeping the ball in front of your chest. 

As you push back up to standing, drive through your front heel and simultaneously press the me

name: Medicine Ball Mountain Climbers
description: Place hands on a medicine ball. Perform mountain climbers by alternately driving knees toward your chest.

name: Medicine Ball Oblique Slam
description:
Hold a medicine ball with both hands. 

Slam the ball down to one side of your body while engaging your obliques, then catch and repeat on the other side.

name: Medicine Ball Oblique Slams Kneeling
description:
Start in a tall kneeling position with your knees on the floor and your core braced. 

Hold a medicine ball with both hands. Twist your torso to one side, raising the ball overhead, then powerfully slam the ball to the ground on the same side. 

Catch the ball, reset, and repeat on the opposite

name: Medicine Ball Over Head Backward Slam
description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, medicine ball on the floor in front of you. Hinge at your hips and slightly bend your knees to grab the ball. 

Lift the ball overhead, fully extending your arms, then engage your core and explosively slam the ball backward toward the floor behind you.

name: Medicine Ball Over Shoulder Toss
description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, medicine ball on the floor in front of you. 

Hinge at your hips and slightly bend your knees to grab the ball. Using your legs, hips, and core, explosively toss the ball over one shoulder behind you. 

Retrieve the ball, return to the starting position

name: Medicine Ball Overhead Lateral Bounds
description:
Hold a medicine ball overhead with arms extended. Jump laterally to one side, landing on one foot, and bring the opposite foot down for balance. 

Immediately jump to the other side. Keep the ball overhead and your movements controlled to engage your core and stabilizers.

name: Medicine Ball Overhead Lunge
description:
1.Stand upright holding a ball in both hands, arms up, extended and straight.

2.Keeping your arms straight upwards, step forward with your one foot in a lunge motion and bend at both knees till they make a 90-degree angle.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat and alternate.

name: Medicine Ball Overhead Side Bend
description:
Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a medicine ball overhead with your arms fully extended. 

Keep your core engaged and your arms straight as you slowly bend your torso to one side, feeling a stretch along your opposite oblique. 

Return to the starting position and repeat o

name: Medicine Ball Pass Crunches
description: Hold a medicine ball at your chest. Perform crunches by lifting your shoulders off the ground and engaging your core.

name: Medicine Ball Pass Extended Crunches
description: Hold a medicine ball overhead. Perform crunches, keeping the ball extended behind you for added resistance.

name: Medicine Ball Pike Push Up
description:
Begin in a pike position with your hips raised and feet close together. 

Place your hands on a medicine ball directly under your shoulders. 

Lower your head toward the ball by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a V-shape, then push back up. 

This targets your shoulders and core stabi

2) You can go to an easier progression such as doing a single leg squat with the TRX straps.

3) Pick up a kettlebell with two hands and hold

name: Medicine Ball Plank
description: Medicine Ball Plank

name: Medicine Ball Plank Roll Outs
description: In a plank position, place forearms on a medicine ball. Roll it forward slightly, then return to start.

name: Medicine Ball Push Up
description:
1.Place your hands on top of the ball and your body at a 45-degree angle with the floor, balancing yourself on the tips of your toes.

2.Bend at the elbows and lower your upper body to a few inches from the ball.

3.Push yourself back up to the starting position.

4.Repeat.

description:
Start in a high plank position with both hands on a medicine ball. Lower your chest into a push-up, then explosively push back up. Immediately drive one knee toward your chest for a single mountain climber. 

On the next rep, perform the push-up again and switch to the opposite leg for the mountai

name: Medicine Ball Rainbows

name: Medicine Ball Reverse Lunge
description:
1.Stand upright holding the ball with both hands, just under the chin and against your chest.

2.Take a step backward, dropping your back knee toward the floor, keeping the ball against your chest.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat and alternate.

2.Twist on the side of your knee that is not touching the floor.

3.Push off your front foot returning to starting position.

Lean back slightly and twist your torso from side to side, tapping the ball on the floor next to each hip.

name: Medicine Ball Shoulder Toss
description:
Hold a medicine ball in front of you at shoulder height. 

Toss the ball to the side, rotating your torso, and catch it as it returns, repeating the motion on both sides.

name: Medicine Ball Sit-Up To Press
description: Perform a sit-up while holding a medicine ball. As you come up, press the ball overhead, then lower back down and repeat.

name: Medicine Ball Slam

"1.Stand upright holding a medicine ball with both hands and stand with your feet at shoulder width.

2.Raise the ball above your head, arms fully extended and straight.

3.Slam the ball into the ground directly in front of you as hard as you can.

4.Catch the ball with both hands on the bounc

name: Medicine Ball Slam with Squat Jump
description:
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a medicine ball overhead. 

Slam the ball down forcefully while bending into a squat. Pick up the ball and immediately jump straight up, holding the ball overhead. 

Land softly and repeat. This combines explosive power and full-body coordination.

name: Medicine Ball Slider Lunge
description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your abdominals on. Ensure that all your lower body segments hit about 90 degrees at the bottom of the lunge.

3.With your feet on the sliders, carry the medicine ball

name: Medicine Ball Squat
description:
1.Stand upright holding the ball with both hands, just under the chin and against your chest.

2.Bend at your knees, putting yourself in a squat position.

3.Straighten legs returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Medicine Ball Squat & Press
description:
1.Holding the ball with both hands, just under the chin and against your chest, take a squat, feet about shoulder-width apart.

2.Stand straight keeping legs straight, as you do this, press the ball up over your head, arms straight and extended.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

name: Medicine Ball Squat & Throw
description:
1.Holding the ball with both hands, just under the chin and against your chest, take a squat, feet about shoulder-width apart.

2.Stand straight keeping legs straight, as you do this, throw the ball up.

3.Return to a squat position, as you do this, catch the ball.

4.Repeat.

name: Medicine Ball Staggered Push Up
description:
1.Keeping your back flat, support your body with your feet on the ground, one hand on a med ball, the other on the floor, arms straight.

2.Lower your body, with your chest almost touching the floor, elbows bent.

3.Push up to a straight arm position.

4.Repeat.

name: Medicine Ball Twist
description:
1.Holding a medicine ball, stand upright with arms extended straight out in front of you.

2.Bring the ball to one side, twisting at your torso, keeping arms straight.

3.Bring the ball to the other side.

4.Repeat.

name: Medicine Ball V-up
description:
Hold a medicine ball in both hands. Bring one knee up towards your chest while snapping the ball towards the knee.

Alternate sides in a controlled motion.

name: Medicine Ball V-Up Pass (Feet to Hands)

As they meet, pass the medicine ball from your hands to your feet, holding it

name: Medicine Ball Windshield Wiper Bent Legs
description: Lie on your back with bent knees holding a medicine ball. Rotate your legs side to side while keeping your upper body stable.

description:

description:
Stand with a mini band around your feet.

Alternate lifting each knee toward your chest while keeping tension on the band.

1. Start by placing the Mini Band around your ankles, and descending into a half-squat position.

3. Repeat this movement, while trying not to move up and

name: Mini Band Around Arms Bear Crawl
description:
Place a mini band around your wrists and get into a bear crawl position with knees hovering.

Crawl forward and backward, keeping tension in the band and maintaining a stable core throughout the movement.

name: Mini Band Backward Fencing Walk
description:
1. Start by placing the band around the arches of both of your feet. 

2. Slightly bend your knees and step one foot backwards while keeping the band tense. 

3. Slowly step your front foot back towards your rear foot, then repeat, travelling backwards.

4. Repeat on the other side.

description:
1) Wrap a Miniband around your thighs, just above the knee.

2) Keep your chest up and knees out as you sit back and down into your hips. Don't let your knees

name: Mini Band Barbell Front Squat
description:
1) Place a miniband around your thighs, just above the knee.

2) With the Barbell right above your chest, set your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Have a slight turn out of your toes. 

3) From there, you‚Äôll sit your hips back, keeping your knees out.

4) Reverse the motion by p

name: Mini Band Bear Crawl (Around Arms)
description:
1. Start with the band looped around both wrists, on all fours, with knees slightly elevated. 

2. In a slow and controlled manner, walk forwards. Reach your opposite arm and leg at the same time, without wagging hips from side to side. 

3. Repeat in reverse, if desired.

name: Mini Band Bear Crawl (Around Legs)
description:
1. Start with the band looped around both knees, on all fours, with knees slightly elevated. 

2. In a slow and controlled manner, walk forwards. Reach your opposite arm and leg at the same time, without wagging hips from side to side. 

3. Repeat in reverse if desired.

name: Mini Band Bent Arm Pull Apart
description:
Hold a mini band with both hands, arms bent at 90 degrees, and elbows close to your sides. 
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Pull the band apart by moving your hands outward while keeping your elbows bent and stable. 

Slowly return to the starting position. 

This exercise targets the sho

name: Mini Band Bent Over Y's
description:
Place a mini band around your wrists and stand with feet hip-width apart. 

Raise your arms overhead in a "Y" shape, keeping tension in the band. Lower your arms back down with control, and repeat the movement.

name: Mini Band Bicycle Crunch
description:
1. Start by lying on your back, with the Mini Band looped around the middle of your feet. 

2. Bring your feet up off the ground, and bend your knees to 90-degrees. 

3. Push one foot away while bringing the opposite foot towards you. Lift your torso up off the ground, and gently rotate towards

name: Mini Band Burpee
description:
1. Place the band around the arches of both your feet. 

2. Reach down to place your hands on the floor, then jump back into a plank position. 

3. Lower your chest to the floor, then push yourself back up the plank.

4. Jump your feet back up towards your hands, stand tall and jump while reac

name: Mini Band Bus Drivers
description: Hold a mini band taut in front of you and twist it side to side as if turning a steering wheel.

name: Mini Band Clamshell
description:
1. Start by placing the band around your thighs, just above the knee. 

2. Lie on your side with your head supported by your arm. 

3. Stack your feet and knees, keeping your knees bent to 90 degrees. 

4. Slowly lift your top knee upwards while keeping your feet together, then slowly return t

name: Mini Band Clamshell

1. Start by lying on your side, with knees bent, and Mini Band looped around your knees.

2. While keeping your heels together, slowly lift your top knee up and away from the floor. 

3. Lower it back down under control without rocking forward or backwards. Repeat on the other side.

name: Mini Band Clamshell Hip Lift with Internal Rotation
